<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093550 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00156: (2026x) Subclassification

- page_id: `298093550`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093550/2026x+Subclassification
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:50:55.626+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Relations > (2026x) Specializations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the subclassification relationshipto specialize a definition element. The specialized definition inherits the features of the more general definition element and can add other features.

##### Subclassification

###### Subclassifying a definition via the Textual Editor

To subclassify a definition via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, identify the definition element that you want to be the specializing one.
2. Follow the element name with either one of the following:
  1. keyword specializes
  2. textual symbol :>
3. Follow the keyword with the name of the specialized definition element.
4. Click the **Synchronize**button. partdefVehicle;partdefDieselVehicle:>Vehicle;/* or */partdefDieselVehiclespecializesVehicle;

###### Subclassifying a definition via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To subclassify a definition via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space='UAF2P'] on an element symbol executing the Set Superclassifier command.

###### Subclassifying a definition via the view palette

To subclassify a definition via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Specializations group, select subclassification .
2. Draw a line with the arrowhead going from the specific definition element to the general one. [ATTACHMENT filename='Subclassification.png']

###### Subclassifying a definitionvia the Create Relationship command

To subclassify a definition via the Create Relationship command

1. Right-click the element that you want to be the specializing one.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relationship , then in the submenu, click Outgoing , and then in the submenu, click subclassification . The Element Picker dialog opens.
3. In the Element Picker dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to specialize. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to specialize and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to specialize from the Containment tree onto the Element Picker .
4. In the Element Picker dialog, click OK .

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094150 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00157: (2026x) Subjects, actors, and objectives for cases

- page_id: `298094150`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094150/2026x+Subjects+actors+and+objectives+for+cases
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:39:11.922+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Analysis & Verification > (2026x) Case
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Once you create the needed case for your model, you can specify it further. Cases are usually specified with:

- The subject of a case is modeled as a distinguished parameter, similarly to the subject of a requirement.
- The objective** of a case is modeled as a requirement usage to be satisfied by the performance of the case.
- A case definition or usage may also have one or more actor parameters that represent roles played by an entity external to the subject of the case but necessary to the specification of the case.

A case can have only one subject and objective.

##### Specifying subject, actors, and objective for case

###### Specifying subjects, actors, and objectives for cases via the Textual Editor

To specify subjects, actors, and objectives for cases via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the curly braces of the case's body, do the following:
  1. To specify a subject, declare the subject keyword, specify the element name , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] (optional).
  2. To specify an actor, declare the actor keyword, specify the element name , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] (optional).
  3. To specify an objective, declare the objective keyword, then in the element body, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Annotations' space='UAF2P'] .
2. Click the Synchronize button. casedefFaultRecovery{// case definition 
subjectsystem:AutomationSystem; // case subject 
actorengineer:Person; // case actor 
objective{ // case objective 
doc 
/* The engineer determines the cause of the systemfault and resolves it returning the system tonominal operation.*/ 
} 
}

###### Specifying subjects, actors, and objectives for cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To specify subjects, actors, and objectives for cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler on a case element symbol executing the **Create Subject,****Create Actor** ,**Create Objective**, or**Create Require Constraint of Objective** command.

###### Specifying subjects, actors, and objectives for cases via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] page.

To specify subjects, actors, and objectives for cases via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select a case symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, select the element you want to create. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.
  - Select a case symbol and on the right side of the actors compartment, click the Create actor button. The new element is created and displayed in the actors compartment.

###### Specifying subjects, actors, and objectives for cases via the Specification panel

To specify subjects, actors, and objectives for cases via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'].
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the needed element kind in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **actors**compartment section's bar, click the **Create actor**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element is created in the appropriate compartment.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<strong>actor </strong>parameters that represent roles played by an entity external to the subject of the case but necessary to the specification of the case.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093749 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00158: (2026x) Subjects, actors, and objectives for use cases

- page_id: `298093749`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093749/2026x+Subjects+actors+and+objectives+for+use+cases
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:55:56.539+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) Use cases
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A use case is typically specified as a sequence of interactions between the subject and the various actors, which are all modeled as part usages. The objective of the use case is to provide an observable result of value to one or more of its actors.

A use case can have only one subject and objective.

##### Specifying subjects, actors, and objectives for use cases

###### Specifying subjects, actors, and objectives for use cases via Textual Editor

To specify subjects, actors, and objectives for use cases via Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the curly braces of the use case's body, do the following:
  1. To specify a subject, declare the subject keyword, specify the element name , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] (optional).
  2. To specify an actor, declare the actor keyword, specify the element name , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] (optional).
  3. To specify an objective, declare the objective keyword, then in the element body , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Annotations' space='UAF2P'] .
2. Click the Synchronize button. usecasedef'Provide Transportation'{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; // use case subject 
actordriver:Person; // use case actor 
actorenvironment:Environment;objective{ // use case objective 
doc/* Transport driver and passengers from starting location to ending location. */ 
}}

###### Specifying subjects, actors, and objectives for use cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To specify subjects, actors, and objectives for use cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space='UAF2P'] on a use case element symbol, executing the **Create Subject,** **Create Actor,** **Create Objective**, or**Create Require Constraint of Objective** command.
- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space='UAF2P'] on the specific subject or actor displayed on the use case element symbol, executing the **Set Subsetted Feature** **** command.

###### Specifying subjects, actors, and objectives for use cases via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] page.

To specify subjects, actors, and objectives for use cases via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select a use case symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, select the element you want to create. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.
  - Select a use case symbol and on the right side of the actors compartment, click the Create actor button. The new element is created and displayed in the actors compartment.

###### Specifying subjects, actors, and objectives for use cases via the Specification panel

To specify subjects, actors, and objectives for use cases via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'].
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the needed element kind in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **actors**compartment section's bar, click the **Create actor**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element is created in the appropriate compartment.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093597 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00159: (2026x) Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements

- page_id: `298093597`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093597/2026x+Subjects+actors+and+stakeholders+for+requirements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:52:06.691+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Once you create the needed requirements for your model, you can specify them further. Requirements are usually specified with subject, actor, and stakeholder parameters.

- A subject is a distinguished parameter that identifies the entity on which the requirement is being specified.
- An **actor**parameter is a part usage representing an entity that plays a special role relative to therequirement definition or usage. A requirement may have multiple actors.
- A **stakeholder**parameter is a part usage representing a role played by an entity (usually a person, organization, or other group) having concerns related to the containing requirement. A requirement may have multiple stakeholders.

##### Specifying subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements

###### Specifying subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements via the Textual Editor

To specify subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the curly braces of the requirement's body, do any of the following:
  1. To specify the subject, declare the subject keyword, specify the element name , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] (optional)
  2. To specify the actor, declare the actor keyword, specify the element name, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] (optional).
  3. To specify the stakeholder, declare the stakeholder keyword, specify the element name , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] (optional).
2. Click the Synchronize button. requirementvehicleSpecification{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
 actor environment : Environment; 
 stakeholder client : Clients;}

###### Specifying subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To specify subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler on a requirement element symbol executing the **Create Actor**,**Create Subject**, or**Create Stakeholder** command.

###### Specifying subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] page.

To specify subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select a requirement symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, select the element you want to create.
  - Select a requirement symbol and on the right side of the needed compartment, click the Create <element> button. The element is created and displayed in the appropriate compartment.

###### Specifying subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements via the Specification panel

To specify subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'].
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the needed element kind in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the needed element compartment's section bar, click the **Create <element>** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element is created in the appropriate compartment.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Once you create the needed requirements for your model, you can specify them further. Requirements are usually specified with subject, actor, and stakeholder parameters.</span></span></p><ul><li>A <strong>subject </strong>is a distinguished parameter that identifies the entity on which the requirement is being specified.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An <strong>actor </strong>parameter is a part usage representing an entity that plays a special role relative to the </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">requirement definition or usage. </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A requirement may have multiple actors.</span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A <strong>stakeholder </strong>parameter is a part usage representing a role played by an entity (usually a person, organization, or other group) having concerns related to the containing requirement. </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A requirement may have multiple stakeholders.</span></span></li></ul><h3>Specifying subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements</h3><h4>Specifying subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">In the Textual Editor, within the curly braces of the requirement's body, do any of the The shortcut menu appears. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the needed element kind in the shortcut menu. <br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the needed element compartment's section bar, click the <strong>Create &lt;element&gt;</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The element is created in the appropriate compartment.</span></li></ol></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094156 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00160: (2026x) Subjects, objectives, and return parameters for analysis cases

- page_id: `298094156`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094156/2026x+Subjects+objectives+and+return+parameters+for+analysis+cases
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:39:27.949+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Analysis & Verification > (2026x) Analysis case
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Once you create the needed analysis case for your model, you can specify it further.An analysis case is comprised of a set of steps with an objective to evaluate a result about a subject of the analysis. Each step can be an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating actions' space='UAF2P'], a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Calculation' space='UAF2P'], or an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Analysis case' space='UAF2P'].

Analysis cases are typically specified with:

- The subject of an analysis case is modeled as a distinguished parameter, similar to the subject of a requirement, identifying what is being analyzed.
- The objective is a requirement on the result of the analysis case, allowing both [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assume constraint' space='UAF2P'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Require constraint' space='UAF2P'] constraints.
- The return parameter for the analysis result, as for a calculation.

A case can have only one subject, objective, and return parameter.

##### Specifying subject, objective, and return parametersfor analysis case

###### Specifying subjects, objectives, and return parameters for analysis cases via the Textual Editor

To specify subjects, objectives, and return parameters for analysis cases via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the curly braces of the case's body, do the following:
  1. To specify a subject, declare the subject keyword, specify the element name , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] (optional).
  2. To specify an objective, declare the objective keyword, then in the element body, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Annotations' space='UAF2P'] .
  3. To specify an analysis result, declare the return keyword followed by the result parameter.
2. Click the Synchronize button. analysisdefFuelEconomyAnalysis{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
returnfuelEconomyResult:DistancePerVolumeValue; 
objectivefuelEconomyAnalysisObjective{ 
doc 
/*The objective of this analysis is to determine whether thesubject vehicle can satisfy the fuel economy requirement.*/ 
requirement:FuelEconomyRequirement; 
} 
// ... 
}

###### Specifying subjects and objectives for analysis cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To specify subjects and objectives for analysis cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler on an analysis case element symbol executing the **Create Subject, Create Objective**, or **Create Require Constraint of Objective** command.

###### Specifying subjects and objectives for analysis cases via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] page.

To specify subjects and objectives for analysis cases via the symbol compartment in a view

- Select an analysis case symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, select the element you want to create. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.

###### Specifying subjects and objectives for analysis cases via the Specification panel

To specify subjects and objectives for analysis cases via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'].
2. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the needed element kind in the shortcut menu. The element is created in the appropriate compartment.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094162 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00161: (2026x) Subjects, objectives, and verdicts for verification cases

- page_id: `298094162`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094162/2026x+Subjects+objectives+and+verdicts+for+verification+cases
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:39:49.097+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Analysis & Verification > (2026x) Verification case
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A verification case is a kind of case whose result is a verdict on whether the subject of the case satisfies certain requirements.

- The subject of a verification case is an input parameter that identifies the system or other entity that is being evaluated as to whether it satisfies certain requirements.
- The objective of a verification case is to verify that the verification subject satisfies one or more specific requirements, which are specified as a special kind of required constraint in the objective.
  - In addition to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Requirement constraints' space='UAF2P'] usages allowed in any requirement usage, the objective of a verification case may also have **requirement verification usages**, which indicate the requirements to be verified by the verification case.
- The return parameter called a verdict, which can be:
  - Pass indicates that the subject has been determined to satisfy the requirements to be verified.
  - Fail indicates that the subject has been determined not to satisfy the requirements to be verified.
  - Inconclusive indicates that a determination could not be made as to whether the subject satisfies the requirements to be verified.
  - Error indicates that an error occurred during the performance of the verification.

- A verification case can have only one subject, objective, and return parameter.
- A verification case always returns a verdict of type VerdictKind (in the library package VerificationCases ), which is an enumeration with allowed values pass , fail , inconclusive , and error .

#### Specifying subject, objective, and verdictfor verification case

###### Specifying subjects, objectives, and verdicts for verification cases via the Textual Editor

To specify subjects, objectives, and verdicts for verification cases via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the curly braces of the case's body, do the following:
  1. To specify a subject, declare the subject keyword, specify the element name , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] (optional).
  2. To specify an objective, declare the objective keyword, then in the element body, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Annotations' space='UAF2P'] .
  3. To specify a verdict, declare the return keyword named verdict, defined by VerdictKind .
2. Click the Synchronize button. verificationdefVehicleMassTest{ 
subjecttestVehicle:Vehicle; 
objectivevehicleMassVerificationObjective{ 
verifyvehicleMassRequirement; 
} 
returnverdict:VerificationCases::VerdictKind; 
}

###### Specifying subjects and objectives for verification cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To specify subjects and objectives for verification cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler on a verification case element symbol executing the **Create Subject**,**Create Objective**, or **Create Require Constraint of Objective** command.

###### Specifying subjects and objectives for verification cases via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] page.

To specify subjects and objectives for verification cases via the symbol compartment in a view

- Select a verification case symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, select the element you want to create. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.

###### Specifying subjects and objectives for verification cases via the Specification panel

To specify subjects and objectives for verification cases via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'].
2. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the needed element kind in the shortcut menu. The element is created in the appropriate compartment.

#### Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective

In addition to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Requirement constraints' space='UAF2P'] usages allowed in any requirement usage, the objective of a verification case may also have **requirement verification usages**, which indicate the requirements to be verified by the verification case. Since the requirement verification usage is a subrequirement of the objective, it is automatically considered a required constraint of its containing objective.

##### Creating new verification case objective-owned requirement verification usages

###### Creating new verification case objective-owned requirement verification usages via the Textual Editor

To create new verification case objective-owned requirement verification usages via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the curly braces of the verification case objective's body, declare the verify requirement keyword, specify the element name, then the requirement body.
2. Click the Synchronize button. verificationdefVehicleMassTest{ 
subjecttestVehicle:Vehicle; 
objectivevehicleMassVerificationObjective{ 
verifyrequirementvehicleMassRequirement{// new verification case objective-owned requirement verification usage 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
inmassActual:>ISQ::mass=vehicle.mass; 
doc/* The vehicle mass shall be less than or equal to 2500 kg. */ 
requireconstraint{ 
massActual<=2500[SI::kg] 
} 
} 
} 
returnverdict:VerificationCases::VerdictKind; 
}

##### Reusing existing requirements as requirement verification usages for a verification case objective

###### Reusing existing requirements as requirement verification usages for a verification case objective via the Textual Editor

Reusing existing requirements as requirement verification usages for a verification case objective via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the curly braces of the verification case objective's body, do one of the following :
  1. To reuse a requirement usage, declare the verify keyword followed by the name of the requirement you want to reuse.
    1. If you want to name the new constraint usage, declare the verify requirement keyword, name the new usage, and then[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space='UAF2P']the requirement you want to reuse.
  2. To reuse a requirement definition, declare the verify requirement keyword, name the new requirement usage (optional), and then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Feature typing' space='UAF2P'] it by the needed requirement definition.
2. (Optional) Specify the requirement in the element's body.
3. Click the Synchronize button. requirementdefVehicleMassRequirement{ 
//... 
}requirementvehicleMassRequirement{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
inmassActual:>ISQ::mass=vehicle.mass; 
doc/* The vehicle mass shall be less than or equal to 2500 kg. */ 
requireconstraint{ 
massActual<=2500[SI::kg] 
} 
} 
verificationdefVehicleMassTest{ 
subjecttestVehicle:Vehicle; 
objectivevehicleMassVerificationObjective{ 
verifyvehicleMassRequirement;// step 1.a. unnamed requirement verification usage reference subsets another requirement usage 
} 
returnverdict:VerificationCases::VerdictKind; 
} 
verificationdefVehicleMassTest{ 
subjecttestVehicle:Vehicle; 
objectivevehicleMassVerificationObjective{ 
verifyrequirementvehicleMassRequirementForTest::>vehicleMassRequirement;// step 1.i. named requirement verification usage reference subsets another requirement usage 
} 
returnverdict:VerificationCases::VerdictKind; 
} 
verificationdefVehicleMassTest{ 
subjecttestVehicle:Vehicle; 
objectivevehicleMassVerificationObjective{ 
verifyrequirementvehicleMassRequirementForTest:VehicleMassRequirement;// step 1.b. named requirement verification usage is defined by a requirement definition 
} 
returnverdict:VerificationCases::VerdictKind; 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">A verification case is a kind of case whose result is a verdict on whether the subject of the case satisfies certain requirements.</span></p><ul><li>The <strong>subject </strong>of a verification case is an input parameter that identifies the system or other entity that is being evaluated as to whether it satisfies certain requirements.</li><li>The <strong>objective </strong>of a verification case is to verify that the verification subject satisfies one or more specific requirements, which are specified as a special kind of required constraint in the objective.<ul><li><p>In addition to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Requirement constraints" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[assumed and required constraint]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> usages allowed in any requirement usage, the objective of a verification case may also have <strong>requirement verification usages</strong>, which indicate the requirements to be verified by the verification case.</p></li></ul></li><li>The return parameter called a verdict, which can be:<ul><li><em>Pass </em>indicates that the subject has been determined to satisfy the requirements to be verified.</li><li><em>Fail </em>indicates that the subject has been determined not to satisfy the requirements to be verified.</li><li><em>Inconclusive </em>indicates that a determination could not be made as to whether the subject satisfies the requirements to be verified.</li><li><em>Error </em>indicates that an error occurred during the performance of the verification.</li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4006e721-ee11-43b3-bc6a-f9a27dcbd813"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>A verification case can have only one subject, objective, and return parameter.</li><li>A verification case always returns a verdict of type <em>VerdictKind</em> (in the library package <em>VerificationCases</em>), which is an enumeration with allowed values <em>pass</em>, <em>fail</em>, <em>inconclusive</em>, and <em>error</em>. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Specifying subject, objective, <span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">and verdict </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">for verification case</span></h2><h4>Specifying subjects, objectives, and verdicts for verification cases via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify subjects, objectives, and verdicts for verification cases via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, within the curly braces of the case's body, do the following:<ol><li>To specify a subject, declare the <em>subject </em>keyword, <span>specify the element name</span>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (optional).</li><li>To specify an objective, declare the <em>objective </em><span>keyword, then in the element body, </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating documentation"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Annotations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[declare a documentation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><br /></span></span></li><li>To specify a verdict, declare the <em>return </em>keyword named verdict, defined by <em>VerdictKind</em>.</li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3887c4b7-aa6f-4315-989f-b1b00b8a9f77"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">verification</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">VehicleMassTest</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">subject</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">testVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">objective</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleMassVerificationObjective</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">verify</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleMassRequirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">return</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">verdict</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">VerificationCases::VerdictKind</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Specifying subjects and objectives for verification cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</h4><p>To specify subjects and objectives for verification cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's<span> </span></span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSML2P/Drag+and+Drop+handler">Drag and Drop handler</a> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">on a verification case <span>element symbol executing the <span><strong>Create Subject</strong>,<strong> Create Objective</strong>, or <strong>Create Require Constraint of Objective </strong></span></span></span>command.</li></ul><h4>Specifying subjects and objectives for verification cases via the symbol compartment in a view</h4><p>For more information, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating elements via compartments"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating elements via compartments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Compartments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p><p>To specify subjects and objectives for verification cases via the symbol compartment in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>Select a verification case symbol and click the<span> </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span> </span>button on it. In the dropdown menu, select the element you want to create. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.</li></ul><h4>Specifying subjects and objectives for verification cases via the Specification panel</h4><p>To specify subjects and objectives for verification cases via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification pane"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section bar, click the <strong>Create Element</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_specification_pane_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Subjects, objectives, and verdicts for verification cases" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The shortcut menu appears. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the needed element kind in the shortcut menu. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The element is created in the appropriate compartment.</span></li></ol><h2>Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective</h2><p>In addition to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Requirement constraints" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[assumed and required constraint]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> usages allowed in any requirement usage, the objective of a verification case may also have <strong>requirement verification usages</strong>, which indicate the requirements to be verified by the verification case. Since the requirement verification usage is a subrequirement of the objective, it is automatically considered a required constraint of its containing objective.</p><h3>Creating new verification case objective-owned <span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">requirement verification usages</span></h3><h4>Creating new verification case objective-owned requirement verification usages via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create new verification case objective-owned requirement verification usages via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the curly braces of the verification case objective's body, declare the <em>verify requirement<span> </span></em>keyword, specify the element name,<span> </span>then the requirement body.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the </span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.<br <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093558 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00162: (2026x) Subsetting

- page_id: `298093558`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093558/2026x+Subsetting
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:51:13.657+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Relations > (2026x) Specializations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the subsetting relationshipto specialize a usage element. Inherited features can be subsetted by multiple features. The specialized definition inherits the features of the more general definition element and can add other features.

##### Subsetting

###### Subsetting a usage via the Textual Editor

To subset a usage via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, identify the usage element that you want to be the subsetting one.
2. Follow the element name with either one of the following:
  1. keyword subsets
  2. textual symbol :>
3. Follow the keyword with the name of the subsetted usage element.
4. Click the Synchronize button. partengine{ 
partvalves; 
} 
partdieselEngine:>engine;/* or */partdieselEnginesubsetsengine;

###### Subsetting a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To subset a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space='UAF2P'] on an element symbol executing the Set Subsetted Feature command.

###### Subsetting a usage via the view palette

To subset a usage via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Specializations group, select subsetting .
2. Draw the line with the arrowhead going from the subsetting usage to the subsetted one. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Subsetting a usage via the Create Relationship command

To subset a usagevia the Create Relationship command

1. Right-click the element that you want to be the subsetting one.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relationship , then in the submenu, click Outgoing , and then in the submenu, click subsetting . The Element Picker dialog opens.
3. In the Element Picker dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to subset. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to subset and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to subset from the Containment tree onto the Element Picker .
4. In the Element Picker dialog, click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093947 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00163: (2026x) Succession

- page_id: `298093947`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093947/2026x+Succession
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-11T20:05:10.392+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A succession is a binary connection that requires its two related usages to have values that are occurrences that happen completely separated in time. A succession asserts that the first action must complete before the second can begin. Successions can be used to assert the ordering of any kind of occurrences in time, but are particularly useful for event occurrences and performances of actions.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The image displays succession connectors, where one action succeeds another.Additionally, the *startTrip*succession connector is also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='UAF2P'].

##### Creating succession usage elements

###### Creating succession usage elements via the Textual Editor

To create succession usage elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the succession keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] (optional), then the keyword first , followed by the name of the first element you want to connect via a succession , then the keyword then , followed by the name of the second element you want to connect. If the succession has no name and no specialization, the keyword *succession*can be omitted.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

50%

// The two related features of a succession are identified after its declaration part and before its body,following the keyword *succession*and separated by the keyword *then*.Actions can be declared separately from the successions:

actiontransportPassenger{ 
actionenterVehicle;// action declarations 
actiondriveVehicle; 
actionstopVehicle; 
actionexitVehicle;

successionstartTripfirstenterVehiclethendriveVehicle;// named successions with source (*first*) and target (*then*) 
successionexecuteTripfirstdriveVehiclethenstopVehicle; 
successionfinishTripfirststopVehiclethenexitVehicle;

// OR

// The keyword *succession* may be omitted for unnamed successions:

firstenterVehiclethendriveVehicle;// unnamed successions with source (*first*) and target (*then*) 
firstdriveVehiclethenstopVehicle; 
firststopVehiclethenexitVehicle; 
}

50%

// Action declarations can be combined with successions:

actiontransportPassenger{ 
actionenterVehicle;// action declaration 
thenactiondriveVehicle;// unnamed successions (*then*) with action declarations (*action <action_name>*) 
thenactionstopVehicle; 
thenactionexitVehicle; 
}

// If a succession is placed lexically directly between the two occurrence usages that are its related elements, then the declaration of the succession can be shortened to just the keyword *then*, prepended to the declaration of the second occurrence usage:

actiontransportPassenger{ 
actionenterVehicle;// action declaration 
firstenterVehiclethendriveVehicle;// unnamed succession with source (*first*) and target (*then*) 
actiondriveVehicle;// action declaration 
firstdriveVehiclethenstopVehicle; 
actionstopVehicle; 
firststopVehiclethenexitVehicle; 
actionexitVehicle; 
}

#### NOTE: Succession shorthands for actions

In addition to the regular full notation for successions, you can also use textual notation succession shorthands within the body of an action definition or usage.

#### EXPAND: Succession shorthands for actions

Succession shorthands for actions

// The source and target actions can be specified separately (first ...; then...;) instead of a single single succession (first ... then...;):

actiontransportPassenger{ 
actionenterVehicle;// action declarations 
actiondriveVehicle; 
actionstopVehicle; 
actionexitVehicle;

firstenterVehicle; 
thendriveVehicle;// unnamed successions 
firstdriveVehicle; 
thenstopVehicle; 
firststopVehicle; 
thenexitVehicle; 
}

// The *then* keyword may also be followed by a complete action usage declaration if source and target actions are specified separately:

actiontransportPassenger{ 
actionenterVehicle; 
firstenterVehicle; 
thenactiondriveVehicle; 
firstdriveVehicle; 
thenactionstopVehicle; 
firststopVehicle; 
thenactionexitVehicle; 
}

// The action preceding the *then* keyword is the source of the succession:

actiontransportPassenger{ 
actionenterVehicle; 
firstenterVehicle; 
thenactiondriveVehicle; 
thenactionstopVehicle; 
thenactionexitVehicle; 
}

###### Creating succession usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

To create succession usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the succession command.
2. Click on the element you want to connect via a succession. A succession path is created between the elements.

###### Creating succession usage elements via the view palette

To create succession usage elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **succession** button to create a succession usage.
2. In a view, click on the first of the two elements you want to connect via a succession, then on the second. A succession is created between the elements.

###### Creating succession usage elements via the Create Relationship command

To create succession usage elements via the Create Relationship command

1. Right-click one of the elements you want to connect via a succession.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relationship , then in the submenu, click Outgoing , and then in the submenu, click succession . The Element Picker dialog opens.
3. In the Element Picker dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to connect. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to connect and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to connect from the Containment tree onto the Element Picker .
4. In the Element Picker dialog, click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
