</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// action declarations</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">succession</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">startTrip</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named successions with source (<em>first</em>) and target (<em>then</em>)</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">succession</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">executeTrip</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">succession</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">finishTrip</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p><p>    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// OR</span></p><p>    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// The keyword <em>succession</em> may be omitted for unnamed successions:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed successions with source (<em>first</em>) and target (<em>then</em>)</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69db49b7-31b6-4886-94d9-e0e835d435ec"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b27dfc04-647d-44c9-8be8-e71cb59d40ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Action declarations can be combined with successions:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">transportPassenger</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// action declaration</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed successions (<em>then</em>) with action declarations (<em>action &lt;action_name&gt;</em>)</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51298067-55c4-406b-a249-e7a463f3a7ac"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// If a succession is placed lexically directly between the two occurrence usages that are its related elements, then the declaration of the succession can be shortened to just the keyword <em>then</em>, prepended to the declaration of the second occurrence usage:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">transportPassenger</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// action declaration</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed succession with source (<em>first</em>) and target (<em>then</em>)</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// action declaration</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f86e499b-ee8a-4b74-854b-6192c8c78f60"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>In addition to the regular full notation for successions, you can also use textual notation succession shorthands within the body of an action definition or usage.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="79e5ce2c-7e23-4ebc-95ee-11da8f27ca3b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Succession shorthands for actions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85429dd3-fe6e-4ae9-9959-9eff70ebfb31"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// The source and target actions can be specified separately (first ...; then...;) instead of a single single succession (first ... then...;):</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">transportPassenger</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// action declarations</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed successions</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// The <em>then</em> keyword may also be followed by a complete action usage declaration i</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">f source and target actions are specified separately</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">transportPassenger</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// The action preceding the <em>then</em> keyword is the source of the succession:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">transportPassenger</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">enterVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driveVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">stopVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">exitVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h4>Creating succession usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view</h4><p>To create succession usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>succession </strong>command.</li><li>Click on the element you want to connect via a succession. A succession path is created between the elements.</li></ol><h4>Creating succession usage elements via the view palette</h4><p>To create succession usage elements via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span> <strong>succession </strong></span><span>button to create a succession usage.</span></li><li>In a view, click on the first of the two elements you want to connect via a succession, then on the second. A succession is created between the elements.</li></ol><h4>Creating succession usage elements <span>via the Create Relationship command</span></h4><p>To create succession usage elements via the Create Relationship command</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click one of the elements you want to connect via a succession.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Relationship</strong>, then in the submenu, click <strong>Outgoing</strong>, and then in the submenu, click <strong>succession</strong>. The <strong>Element Picker </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Element Picker </strong>dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>In a view, click the element symbol you want to connect. Hold Shift to select multiple elements. </li><li>Click the <strong>Add <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Succession" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong>button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to connect and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Drag and drop the needed element you want to connect from the Containment tree onto the <strong>Element Picker</strong>.</li></ol></li><li>In the <strong>Element Picker</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093957 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00164: (2026x) Succession flow

- page_id: `298093957`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093957/2026x+Succession+flow
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:42:55.110+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A succession flow is a transfer that only occurs after the source action ends and before the target action starts. A succession flow connection is specified in the same way as a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Flow' space='UAF2P'], but it adds the further constraint that the transfer source must complete before the transfer starts, and the transfer must complete before the target can start.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The image displays the *lightFlow* succession flow connector defined by *LightFlow*from *produceDirectedLight.light*to*reflectLight.light***with the payload *light*typed by *Light*.Additionally, the connector is also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='UAF2P'].

##### Creating succession flow usage elements

###### Creating succession flow usage elements via the Textual Editor

To create succession flow usage elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the succession flow keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Succession flow' space='UAF2P'] (optional), then the from keyword and the name of the source element, followed by the keyword to and the name of the target element . If the succession flow has no name and no specialization, the keyword *from*can be omitted.
3. Click the Synchronize button. successionflowlightFlow:LightFlowfromproduceDirectedLight.lighttoreflectLight.light; // named and specializedsuccession flowsuccessionflow:LightFlowfromproduceDirectedLight.lighttoreflectLight.light; // unnamed specializedsuccession flowsuccessionflowlightFlowfromproduceDirectedLight.lighttoreflectLight.light; // named unspecializedsuccession flowsuccessionflowproduceDirectedLight.lighttoreflectLight.light; // unnamed unspecializedflow can omit the keyword *from*

###### Creating succession flow usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

To create succession flow usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the succession flow command.
2. Click on the element you want to connect via a succession flow. A succession flow path is created between the elements.

###### Creating succession flow usage elements via the view palette

To create succession flow usage elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **succession flow** button to create a succession flow usage.
2. In a view, click on the source element. A dropdown menu appears. Select the output element in the menu.
3. Then click on the target element. A dropdown menu appears. Select the compatible input element in the menu. A flow usage is created between the elements.

##### Setting the payload for the flow

While the payload name and sign are displayed on the flow in a view by default, you can hide them using the **Show Payload** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space='UAF2P'].

###### Setting the payload for the succession flow via the Textual Editor

To set the payload for the succession flow via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor before the keyword *from*within the succession flow for which you want to set a payload.
2. Declare the keyword of followed by either of the following:
  1. The name of the payload specialized by a definition element.
  2. The name of the definition element you want to set as payload for the flow.
3. Click the Synchronize button. successionflow:LightFlowofLightfromproduceDirectedLight.lighttoreflectLight.light; // specialized(feature typing) succession flow with payload set as a definition elementsuccession flow lightFlow : LightFlow of light : Light from produceDirectedLight.light to reflectLight.light; // specialized(feature typing) succession flow with a named and specialized (feature typed) payload

###### Setting the payload for the succession flow via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To set the payload for the succession flow via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop a definition element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Succession flow' space='UAF2P'] on a succession flow element symbol executing the Set Payload command.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">payload</span></span></span></span></span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Setting the payload for the succession flow via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</h4><p>To set the payload for the succession flow via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Drag and drop a definition element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's<span> </span></span><ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Drag and Drop handler]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Succession flow" /></ac:link> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">on a succession flow element <span>symbol executing the </span></span><strong>Set Payload </strong>command.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093848 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00165: (2026x) Swimlane grid

- page_id: `298093848`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093848/2026x+Swimlane+grid
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:37:42.021+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) Actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The swimlane grid symbol allows you to model and organize actions based on the elements responsible for performing those actions.Each swimlane in the swimlane grid representsthe system element responsible for performing actions displayed in the corresponding swimlane. The represented elements are easily identifiable as their names are indicated in the headers of each swimlane with the keyword «performer», indicating their role of beingresponsible for performing actions displayed in their corresponding swimlanes.

The swimlane grid symbol is comprised of two sections:

- The swimlane headers display each swimlane referent's name, i.e., the names of the elements responsible for performing actions displayed in the specific swimlanes.
- The swimlane pool is comprised of individual swimlanes . The swimlane pool allows you to easily model actions and their sequencing based on their performers, i.e., the elements that perform these actions, displayed as separate swimlanes.
  - The individual swimlanes display actions performed by the swimlane referents whose names are indicated in the swimlane headers with the keyword «performer» .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The swimlane grid symbol can be displayed for the following elements:

- perform action . Its swimlanes represent the **subparts** of the part that owns and/or inherits the perform action. The perform action element symbol owns the swimlane grid symbol.
  - For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modeling actions with swimlanes' space='UAF2P'] page.
- include use case . Its swimlanes represent the subject and actor parameters owned and/or inherited by the include use case. The include use case element symbol owns the swimlane grid symbol.
  - For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Use case scenario' space='UAF2P'] page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Examples of the swimlane grid symbol in include use case and perform action element symbols. 
The *find person in distress* include use case's subject and actors are represented by the swimlanes. 
The *drone in any mission* part's subparts are represented by the swimlanes displayed for the perform action owned by the containing part.

##### Working with the swimlane grid

To perform the following procedures, the perform action or the include use case that contains the actions (or would contain if they are not created yet)performed by swimlane referents must be displayed in the view.

###### Displaying swimlane grid and creating additional swimlanes

To display swimlane grid and create additional swimlanes

- To display the swimlane grid:
  - Right-click the perform action/include use case symbol and in the shortcut menu, click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='UAF2P'] , then Display Swimlanes . The swimlane grid is displayed with swimlanes (along with any actions and connectors that may already be defined in the model), representing elements owned or inherited by the element that owns or inherits the perform action/include use case.
- To create additional swimlanes:
  - Select the header of an existing swimlane and click the + button on the left or the right side of the swimlane, to create a swimlane to the left or the right of the existing one. Creating a new swimlane creates:
    - a subpart for the element that owns or inherits the perform action, represented by the new swimlane.
    - an actor for the include use case, represented by the new swimlane. However, no related subpart is created for the part that owns or inherits the include use case.

###### Displaying actions in swimlanes

To display actions in swimlanes

- Select one swimlane by right-clicking its header or all swimlanes by right-clicking the swimlane pool. In the shortcut menu, click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='UAF2P']**Display** , then Display Actions in the dropdown menu . The specific swimlane's shortcut menu contains the same commands as the element symbol it represents.

###### Displaying parameters, connectors, or other features for element symbols in swimlanes

To display parameters, connectors, or other features for element symbols in swimlanes

- Right-click the element symbol(s) in the swimlane(s). In the shortcut menu, click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='UAF2P']**Display** , then click the appropriate command in its dropdown menu.

###### Rearranging swimlanes

To rearrange swimlanes

- Click the header of a swimlane, then the displayed control arrows to move the swimlane left or right. [ATTACHMENT filename='Rearranging_Swimlanes.png']

###### Moving the swimlane grid symbol

To move the swimlane grid symbol

1. Click anywhere on the swimlane pool. The **Move** icon at the top left of the swimlane grid symbol appears.
2. Click the Move icon and move the swimlane grid symbol. [ATTACHMENT filename='Moving_Swimlanes.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The represented elements are easily identifiable as their names are indicated in the headers of each swimlane with the keyword «performer», indicating their role of being </span><span>responsible for performing actions displayed in their corresponding swimlanes. </span></p><p><span>The swimlane grid symbol is comprised of two sections:</span></p><ul><li>The <strong>swimlane headers</strong> display each swimlane referent's name, i.e., the names of the elements<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> responsible for performing actions displayed in the specific swimlanes.</span></li><li>The <strong>swimlane pool</strong> is comprised of <strong>individual swimlanes</strong>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298092859 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00166: (2026x) Switching to System Engineer SysML v2 perspective

- page_id: `298092859`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298092859/2026x+Switching+to+System+Engineer+SysML+v2+perspective
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T20:45:25.719+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Modeling tool environment
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When working with SysML v2 features, you should switch the modeling tool's perspective to the System Engineer SysML v2 perspective.

Generally, when you create a new SysML v2 project, a question dialog is prompted, asking you to switch the perspective. Clicking Yes changes the modeling tool's perspective to the System Engineer SysML v2 perspective.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Otherwise, see the procedures below on how to switch the perspective.

##### Switching to the System Engineer SysML v2 perspective

To switch to the System Engineer SysML v2 perspective

- Do one of the following:
  - In the main menu, select**Options**>**Perspectives**>**Perspectives**. In the**Select Perspective**dialog, select the**System Engineer SysML v2**perspective, and click the**Apply**button.
  - In the main toolbar, click the**Perspective**drop-down box and select the**System Engineer SysML v2**perspective.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When working with SysML v2 features, you should switch the modeling tool's perspective to the System Engineer SysML v2 perspective. </p><p>Generally, when you create a new SysML v2 project, a question dialog is prompted, asking you to switch the perspective. Clicking Yes changes the modeling tool's perspective to the System Engineer SysML v2 perspective.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="question_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Switching to System Engineer SysML v2 perspective" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Otherwise, see the procedures below on how to switch the perspective.</p><h3>Switching to the System Engineer SysML v2 perspective</h3><p>To switch to the System Engineer SysML v2 perspective</p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Do one of the following:</span></span><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In the main menu, select<span> </span><strong>Options<span> </span></strong>&gt;<span> </span><strong>Perspectives<span> </span></strong>&gt;<span> </span><strong>Perspectives</strong>. In the<span> </span><strong>Select Perspective</strong><span> </span>dialog, select the<span> </span><strong>System Engineer SysML v2</strong><span> </span>perspective, and click the<span> </span><strong>Apply<span> </span></strong>button.</span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In the main toolbar, click the<span> </span><strong>Perspective</strong><span> </span>drop-down box and select the<span> </span><strong>System Engineer SysML v2</strong><span> </span>perspective.</span></span></li></ul></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094100 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00167: (2026x) Symbol styles

- page_id: `298094100`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094100/2026x+Symbol+styles
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:50:39.862+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Element symbols, including shapes and paths, are displayed on views based on the symbol styles defined in the style sheets in the *3DS SysML Customization*library that is loaded in every project. You can modify element symbol styles according to your preferences by working with style sheets.

Types of style sheet specification:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Predefined style sheets' space='UAF2P'] Each SysML v2 project is automatically loaded with the*3DS SysML Customization* library that contains predefined style sheets comprised of sets of rules defining symbol styles based on element metaclass. The *DefaultStyleSheet*style sheet contains model-based styles that apply to all element symbols in SysML v2 projects by default based on element metaclass.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom style sheets' space='UAF2P'] Instead of or in addition to using the existing style sheets, you can also create custom style sheets with your own sets of rules for symbol styles.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space='UAF2P'] Whether using a default or a custom style, y ou can customize the styles of individual symbols. Using the Symbol Style dialog, you can specify the style properties for specific symbols, determining their appearance. Changing the styles for an individual symbol via the Symbol Style dialog overwrites predefined and custom model-based symbol styles; the modifications persist even after updating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating style sheets' space='UAF2P'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual views' space='UAF2P'] Whether using a default or a custom style, y ou can customize the styles of individual views. Using the View Style dialog, you can specify the style properties for specific views, determining their appearance. Changing the styles for an individual view via the View Style dialog overwrites predefined and custom model-based symbol styles; the modifications persist even after updating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating style sheets' space='UAF2P'] .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### On the left: symbol styles using a predefined style sheet *DefaultStyleSheet*. 
In the middle: symbol styles using a predefined style sheet*DefaultStyleSheet*with the customized style for the individual attribute symbol. 
On the right: symbol styles using a custom style sheet.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

You can modify element symbol styles according to your preferences by working with style sheets. </span></p><p>Types of style sheet specification:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Predefined style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Predefined style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Each SysML v2 project is automatically loaded with the<span> <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">3DS SysML Customization</span></em></span> library that contains predefined style sheets comprised of sets of rules defining symbol styles based on element metaclass. The <em>DefaultStyleSheet </em>style sheet contains </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">model-based styles that apply to all element symbols in SysML v2 projects by default based on element metaclass. </span><br /></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Custom style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br />Instead of or in addition to using the existing style sheets, you can also create custom style sheets with your own sets of rules for symbol styles. </li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Modifying styles of individual symbols]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Whether using a default or a custom style, y</span></span>ou can customize the styles of individual symbols. Using the View Style dialog, you can specify the style properties for specific views, determining their appearance. Changing the styles for an individual view via the View Style dialog overwrites predefined and custom model-based symbol styles; the modifications persist even after updating <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Activating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[active style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="symbol_styles.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Symbol styles" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">On the left: symbol styles using a predefined style sheet <em>DefaultStyleSheet</em>.<br />In the middle: <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">symbol styles using a predefined style sheet </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">DefaultStyleSheet </em>with the customized style for the individual attribute symbol.<br />On the right: symbol styles using a custom style sheet.</h6>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093219 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00168: (2026x) Symbolic views

- page_id: `298093219`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093219/2026x+Symbolic+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-17T14:53:37.426+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model management > (2026x) Working with views > (2026x) View kinds > (2026x) Graphical views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Symbolic views allow you to model via symbolic model representation.In a symbolic view, elements are represented as shapes, and relationships and connectors as paths, both shapes and paths are referred to as symbols.Youcan use symbolic views for modeling and representing parts interconnection, action flow, requirements, etc.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays a standard predefined symbolic view in the View Editor tab with the toolbar, pane, and palette. Elements and connectors are displayed as shapes and paths on the pane.

##### Symbolic views

Specific symbolic views you create in the modeling tool are predefined symbolic view usages defined by appropriate predefined symbolic view definitions. These predefined symbolic view definitions can differ in the palettes they provide, renderings and/or element filters they specify.

- For symbolic view creation procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating symbolic views' space='UAF2P'] page.
- Additionally, you can create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P'] .

Available symbolic view definitions are differentiated as follows:

- **Standard predefined symbolic view definitions**
  - Do not have any specified filters, meaning, any elements can be exposed by these views. Once you have created such a view, you may want to specify [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Filtering elements for views' space='UAF2P'] to limit the displayed elements.
  - Can be created via the Textual Editor or the Create View command in the Containment tree.
- Predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions
  - Contain element filters specified in the*DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression**::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter*and *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression**::***EssentialElementsFilter**view definitions, narrowing down what elements can be exposed by the view by discarding various standard library elements and other non-essential elements that are unlikely to be relevant for display in views.
  - Additionally, most of the predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions contain additional filters to narrow down the exposed elements, e.g., *PartsTreeView*view definition contains filters specifying that only part usage elements can be exposed.
  - Can be created only via the Textual Editor.

See the table below listing all predefined symbolic view definitions.

| View definitions | Description | Palette | Availability |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Standard predefined symbolic view definitions |  |  |  |
| General View | Standard predefined multi-purpose general symbolic view for element representation and modeling. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. | Via Textual EditorVia the Create View command dialog/submenu |
| Interconnection View | Standard predefined symbolic view for element interconnection representation and modeling. | Includes a view palette with button categories optimized for interconnections modeling. |  |
| Action Flow View | Standard predefined symbolic view for action flow representation and modeling. | Includes a view palette with button categories optimized for action flow modeling. |  |
| State Transition View | Standard predefined symbolic view for state transition representation and modeling. | Includes a view palette with button categories optimized for state transition modeling. |  |
| Predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions |  |  |  |
| TreeView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display common elements in tree-view rendering style. It subclassifies the NonStandardLibraryElementFilter and EssentialElementsFilter view definitions, which include filters to exclude various standard library elements and other non-essential elements that are unlikely to be relevant for display in views. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. | Via Textual Editor |
| NestedView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display common elements in nested-view rendering style.It subclassifies the NonStandardLibraryElementFilter and EssentialElementsFilter view definitions, which include filters to exclude various standard library elements and other non-essential elements that are unlikely to be relevant for display in views. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. |  |
| UsagesNestedView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display usage elements in nested-view rendering style.It subclassifies the NestedView view definition. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. |  |
| PartsNestedView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display parts as nested shapes, with the connections between them represented as paths.It subclassifies the UsagesNestedView view definition. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. |  |
| Parts&PortsNestedView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display parts and ports as nested shapes, with the connections between them represented as paths.It subclassifies the UsagesNestedView view definition. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. |  |
| PartsTreeView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display parts in a decomposition tree style.It subclassifies the TreeView view definition. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. |  |
| ActionsNestedView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display actions as nested shapes, parameters as shapes on border, and in-between connections represented as paths.It subclassifies the UsagesNestedView view definition. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. |  |
| ActionsTreeView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display actions in a decomposition tree style. It subclassifies the TreeView view definition. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. |  |
| RequirementTreeView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display the exposed requirements in a decomposition tree style. It subclassifies the TreeView view definition. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. |  |
| StatesNestedView | Predefined symbolic view definition that uses expressions to display states as nested shapes, with transitions between them represented as paths.It subclassifies the UsagesNestedView view definition. | Includes a view palette with all available button categories. |  |

##### Symbolic view areas

Views are displayed in the View Editor tab.

- Toolbar allows you to quickly access the options for managing element symbols in the view. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Symbolic views' space='UAF2P'] subsection.
- Pane allows you to model and represent elements as symbols. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='UAF2P'] page.
- Palette allows you to model elements. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Symbolic views' space='UAF2P'] subsection.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Symbolic view palette

The palette contains buttons for the majority of the elements you can create in the view pane, as well as additional buttons for working with symbols in the view pane.

- The buttons are ordered into categories: Common, Items/Ports/Attributes, Connectors, Specializations, Other Relationships, Actions, Other Actions, States, Cases, Requirements/Constraints. Click the category title to expand the contained element buttons.
- Some element buttons have a down-facing arrow button allowing you to view the button's submenu of other element kinds (e.g., part and part def or in ref , in item , and in attribute ).
- The available buttons and their categories depend on the symbolic view definition.
- You can create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P'] .

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']View palette's Items/Ports/Attributes category with the *part*button's submenu.

###### Symbolic view toolbar buttons

The table below shows the buttons available in the symbolic view toolbar.

| Button | Shortcut keys | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Navigation toolbar |  |  |
| Select in Containment tree | Alt+B | Click the button to highlight the element of the selected symbol in the Containment tab of the Model Browser. |
| Layout toolbar |  |  |
| Quick Diagram Layout | Ctrl+Q | Click the arrow button to display the dropdown menu. In it, select the Layout SysML v2 Style option. |
| Align | - | Click the button to display the dropdown menu with the symbol alignment options. |
| Reset Label Positions | - | Click the button to reset the labels' position to default. Make sure the labels are selected first. |
| Edit toolbar |  |  |
| Copy | Ctrl+C | Click to copy the selected symbol. |
| Paste | Ctrl+V | Click to paste the copied symbol. |
| Delete | Ctrl+D | Click to remove selected elements both from the view and the model. |
| Remove From Diagram | Delete | Click to remove the selected element from the view. |
| Shape Editing toolbar |  |  |
| Make Preferred Size | - | Click to resize the selected symbol(s) to the most compact size. |
| Display toolbar |  |  |
| Display | - | Click the arrow button to display the dropdown menu. In that menu, select the kind of symbols you want to display in the view for the selected symbol. You can also choose the style in which the symbols can be displayed, i.e., tree-view or nested. |
| Style Sheets toolbar |  |  |
| Style Sheets | - | Click to activate style sheets for the view or the entire project. >]]> |
| Other buttons |  |  |
| Back | Alt+Left Arrow | Click to navigate to the previously opened view. |
| Forward | Alt+Right Arrow | Click to navigate to the previously opened view. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Symbolic views allow you to model via symbolic model representation. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In a symbolic view, elements are represented as shapes, and relationships and connectors as paths, both shapes and paths are referred to as symbols. </span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">You </span>can use symbolic views for modeling and representing <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">parts interconnection, action flow, requirements, etc</span>. </span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="symbolic_view_example.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Symbolic views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays a standard predefined symbolic view in the View Editor tab with the toolbar, pane, and palette. Elements and connectors are displayed as shapes and paths on the pane.  </h6><h3>Symbolic views</h3><p>Specific symbolic views you create in the modeling tool are predefined symbolic view usages defined by appropriate predefined symbolic view definitions. These predefined symbolic view definitions can differ in the palettes they provide, renderings and/or element filters they specify. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2cfe40a7-b52d-49a2-98b4-83b0292b7f7c"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span>For symbolic view creation procedures, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating symbolic views]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></li><li><span>Additionally, you can create </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom symbolic view definitions with customized palettes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Available symbolic view definitions are differentiated as follows:</p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Standard predefined symbolic view definitions</strong> </span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Do not have any specified filters, meaning, any elements can be exposed by these views. Once you have created such a view, you may want to specify <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Filtering elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to limit the displayed elements.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Can be created via the Textual Editor or the Create View command in the Containment tree.</span></li></ul></li><li><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions</span></strong><ul><li data-uuid="ad2b1ac9-2819-4de9-aacf-e253ec9ca4b1"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Contain element filters specified in the<span> <em>DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression</em></span><em>::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter<span> </span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">and <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><em>DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression</em></span><em>::</em></span></span><em><span><em style="text-align: left;">EssentialElementsFilter </em></span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">view definitions</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">, narrowing down what elements can be exposed by the view by discarding various standard library elements and other non-essential elements that are unlikely to be relevant for display in views. </span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Additionally, most of the predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions contain additional filters to narrow down the exposed elements, e.g., <em>PartsTreeView<span> </span></em>view definition contains filters specifying that only part usage elements can be exposed.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Can be created only via the Textual Editor.</span></li></ul></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the table below listing all predefined symbolic view definitions.</span></p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 88.1841%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 14.4643%;" /><col style="width: 53.4242%;" /><col style="width: 20.4617%;" /><col style="width: 11.642%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" style="text-align: center;" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"><strong title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">View definitions</span></strong></td><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" style="text-align: center;" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"><strong title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" selected symbol(s) to the most compact size. </p></div></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="blue"><strong>Display toolbar</strong></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Symbolic views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Display</p></div></td><td><span>-</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click the arrow button to display the dropdown menu. In that menu, select the kind of symbols you want to display in the view for the selected symbol. You can also choose the style in which the symbols can be displayed, i.e., tree-view or nested.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><div class="content-wrapper" title=""><p><strong>Style Sheets toolbar</strong></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="stylesheetsbutton.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Symbolic views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Style Sheets</p></div></td><td><span>-</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to activate style sheets for the view or the entire project. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Activating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></div></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-blue" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="blue"><strong>Other buttons</strong></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Back" ac:width="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="back_diagram_toolbar.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Symbolic views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Back</p></div></td><td>Alt+Left Arrow</td><td><p>Click to navigate to the previously opened view.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Forward" ac:width="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="forward_diagram_toolbar.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Symbolic views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Forward</p></div></td><td><p>Alt+Right Arrow</p></td><td><p>Click to navigate to the previously opened view.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094125 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00169: (2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications

- page_id: `298094125`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094125/2026x+Synchronizing+custom+style+sheet+modifications
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:52:26.710+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View > (2026x) Symbol styles > (2026x) Custom style sheets
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Once you have created a style sheet and either customized the *DefaultStyleSheet* components or created custom ones, you need to synchronize the modifications in the Textual Editor and update all views for the modifications to be reflected in model views. Make sure to check whether you also need to assess layout-sensitive changes, as otherwise the updated styles will not be implemented.

##### Synchronizing modifications

Synchronization and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='UAF2P'] do not affect customizations made to individual symbols via the **Symbol Style** dialog. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space='UAF2P'] procedure on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space='UAF2P'] page.

To synchronize modifications

1. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Textual Editor's toolbar, click the**Synchronize**button[IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  2. Use the Alt+S shortcut keys.
2. Ensure the new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating style sheets' space='UAF2P'].
3. Press the **F5**key on the keyboard to update all affected views.
4. (Conditional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='UAF2P']. You need to assess layout-sensitive changes if you already have views in your project and the style sheet contains updated values of the following attributes:General style attributes: *font*, *wrap, textVerticalPosition, textHorizontalPosition, lineStyle, orientation, isRounded, pathStyle, showPayload.*Compartment style attributes:*showCompartment, showShortName,showName, showFullText, showSpecialization, showQualifiedNameForSpecialization, showModifiers, showValue, showInheritedSign, showInherited, showOnlyOwned.*

##### Assessing layout-sensitive changes

Certain style attributes are layout-sensitive as changing their values can affect already-created views' layout. Whether you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating style sheets' space='UAF2P'] a new one if the updated style sheet contains styles with attribute values that are different from those that were used prior, you need to follow the procedure below to assess and either confirm or deny the changes.

Omitting this procedure means the modeling tool will not implement the updates of the new style and retain the previous attribute values representing symbols in the old style.

Follow the procedure below if you already have views in your project and the style sheet contains updated values of the following attributes:

- General style attributes: *font*, *wrap, textVerticalPosition, textHorizontalPosition, lineStyle, orientation, isRounded, pathStyle, showPayload.*
- Compartment style attributes: showCompartment, showShortName,showName, showFullText, showSpecialization, showQualifiedNameForSpecialization, showModifiers, showValue, showInheritedSign, showInherited, showOnlyOwned.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Symbol Style dialog displays the highlighted rows of the updated attributes after activating a new style sheet within which the values of layout-sensitive attributes are updated. 
At the top of the dialog, a notification provides commands for either using the new style or retaining the old one.

###### Assessing layout-sensitive changes

To assess layout-sensitive changes

1. In a view, right-click an affected symbol. The procedure must be performed for each affected symbol individually.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Symbol Style . The Symbol Style dialog opens.
3. Review the affected attributes in the highlighted rows. The rows display the old attribute values.
4. In the notification at the top of the dialog, click one of the following commands:
  1. Use the new style - implements the updated attribute values and displays the symbol in the new style.
  2. Retain the old style - does not implement the updated attribute values and displays the symbol in the old style. Undoing the commandTo undo the **Retain the old style** command for a selected attribute: 
If you retain the old style, the check boxes in the **Inherited**column are deselected because the affected attributes are not inherited from the current style sheet and are considered individually modified.You can undo the retained style and switch to the new one for the specific attribute by selecting the check box in the **Inherited**column for the appropriate attribute.To undo either the **Retain the old style**or**Use the new style**command for all attributes, do one of the following:Press **Ctrl+Z** on the keyboard.In the **main menu**, click **Edit**> **Undo**.
5. Click Close .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00170: (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation

- page_id: `298092803`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298092803/2026x+SysML+v2+Plugin+Documentation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-01-30T13:29:23.355+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition is out now!

INLINE

#### NOTE: CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition is out now!

false

CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition is out now!

Explore our SysML v2 solution with the free **CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition**. Click [here](https://discover.3ds.com/free-catia-sysmlv2-community-edition) to learn more.

SysML v2 is a next-generation systems modeling language. Compared to SysML v1, SysML v2 intends to provide improved precision, expressiveness, and usability of the modeling language.

To fully take advantage of SysML v2, two primary plugins are available for you:

- The SysML v2 Plugin provides core SysML v2 features and includes the SysML v2 Textual Editor, enabling you to model using both textual and graphical notations.
- The [SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2EP/?contextKey=sysml-v2-evaluation-plugin-documentation&version=2026x) allows you to conduct static evaluations of SysML v2 models .

For more information, please see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Prerequisites' space='UAF2P'] page.

For your convenience, you can download the **SysML v2 Quick Sheet** as a concise reference to the SysML v2 language on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML v2 Quick Sheet' space='UAF2P'] page.

The following topics explain how to install and use the SysML v2 Plugin:

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
