<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298090792 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00202: Alpha 1.5 Version News

- page_id: `298090792`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298090792/Alpha+1.5+Version+News
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-15T09:45:49.165+02:00`
- ancestors: 
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### SysML v2 Plugin

Released on:October 8, 2024

The SysML v2 Alpha 1.5 release is a technology preview that will allow you to evaluate some of the SysML v2 features that have been implemented.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.5 Version News' space='UAF2P']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.5 Version News' space='UAF2P']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.5 Version News' space='UAF2P']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.5 Version News' space='UAF2P']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.5 Version News' space='UAF2P']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.5 Version News' space='UAF2P']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.5 Version News' space='UAF2P']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.5 Version News' space='UAF2P']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.5 Version News' space='UAF2P']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.5 Version News' space='UAF2P']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.5 Version News' space='UAF2P']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.5 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.5 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.5 Version News' space='UAF2P']

#### Full Support for SysML v2 in the Textual Editor

The SysML v2 Textual Editor now fully supports all concepts of the SysML v2 modeling language, providing 100% coverage.

Explore every aspect of SysML v2 with confidence, as our editor meets all your modeling needs — from requirements, structure, and behavior to analysis cases, verification cases, and more.

Please note, filter conditions are not applicable for views.

#### Rule-Based Styles

The Alpha 1.5 version introduces new and improved rule-based styles functionality, including:

- Predefined rule-based styles . Now the Symbol Styles library comes with two predefined style sheets: the improved DefaultStyle style sheet with updated rules and styles as well as the new WhiteAndBlackStyle style sheet that contains rule-based styles for representing all element symbols in black and white style. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Predefined style sheets' space='UAF2P']>]]> [ATTACHMENT filename='predefined_rule_based_styles.png']

###### Symbol styles represented by the *DefaultStyle*(left) and *WhiteAndBlackStyle*(right) style sheets.

- Custom rule-based styles . Instead of using the predefined styles, you can now create style sheets with your own rule-based styles either by reusing and customizing the *DefaultStyle* style sheet or creating a custom style sheet from scratch. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom style sheets' space='UAF2P']>]]> [IMAGE alt='' src='']The predefined rule-based symbol styles using the *DefaultStyle*style sheet (left) and an example of custom rule-based symbol styles using a custom-created style sheet (right).
- Layout-sensitive changes assessment . The Symbol Properties dialog has been enhanced to track style attribute changes that can affect already-created diagrams' layout. Easily assess the highlighted properties and either confirm or deny the changes. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assessing layout-sensitive changes' space='UAF2P']>]]> [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Symbol Properties dialog displays the highlighted rows of layout-sensitive attributes whose values were updated in the style sheet. 
At the top of the dialog, a notification provides commands for either using the new style or retaining the old one. 
The image displays the updated *driver*part usage element symbol once the 'Use the new style' command is selected in the dialog notification.
- Style sheets management . The Active Style Sheets dialog allows you to manage all existing style sheets, both predefined and custom, determining which one(s) should be activated for the project. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activating style sheets' space='UAF2P']>]]> 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Activating the *WhiteAndBlackStyle*style sheet instead of the *DefaultStyle*via the Active Style Sheets dialog.

#### Specification Pane

The Alpha 1.5 version introduces the Specification pane that can be opened for any element in the project. It allows you to review and modify properties of specific elements, such as the declared name, declared short name, type, value, and documentation. You can also review, modify, or createowned elements in the appropriate compartments, which are equivalent to those displayed in diagram symbols.[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space='UAF2P']>]]>

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Specification pane displays the *vehicleMassLimitationRequirement*element's specification information.

#### States

The new tool version now supports state modeling, enhancing the ability to accurately represent dynamic system behavior and improving clarity and consistency in behavioral modeling. This includes:

- State modeling , enabling the creation of state usage and state definition elements, with support for special actions like entry, do, and exit actions. Learn more [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating states' space='UAF2P']>]]> and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Entry, do, and exit actions for states' space='UAF2P']>]]>
- Parallel state specification for modeling concurrent behaviors. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Parallel states' space='UAF2P']>]]>
- State transitions , allowing you to specify movement between states with the ability to add triggers, guard conditions, and effect actions. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='UAF2P']>]]>
- Exhibit state specification , which lets you specify elements that exhibit specific states. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exhibit state' space='UAF2P']>]]>

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The image displays a *Vehicle* part which exhibits the *operationalStates*state machine. The states include special actions like entry, do, and exit actions. The states are connected via transitions, which also contain triggers, guard conditions and effect actions.

#### Allocations

The new release allows you to create allocations where the responsibility for realizing the intent of the source is allocated to the target, defining traceable links as a kind of mapping across the various structures and hierarchies of a system model.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocation' space='UAF2P']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays a *torqueGenAlloc* allocation usage feature typed by the *LogicalToPhysical*allocation definition. The allocation allocates the *torqueGenerator*part usage to the *powerTrain* part usage. Additionally, the allocation connector is also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='UAF2P'].

#### Displaying Relationships

The Display commands now include two new categories — Relationships and In-Between Relationships — for displaying relationships between element symbols on a diagram. The Relationships category contains commands for displaying the relationship paths between the selected symbol and all other symbols displayed on the diagram. The In-Between Relationships category contains commands for displaying relationship paths only between the selected symbols. The commands in these categories allow you to display all types of connectors, specializations, and other relationships (e.g., annotations, memberships, etc.).

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='UAF2P']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### On the left: the 'Display Other' command from the In-Between Relationships display category is executed for two symbols selected on the diagram, displaying the relationship (feature membership)between them.On the right: the 'Display Other' command from the Relationships display category is executed for the selected symbol, displaying its relationships (feature memberships) with other symbols displayed on the diagram.

#### Copying and Pasting Elements

Now you can copy, paste, cut, and delete elements using the appropriate commands in the element shortcut menu in the Containment tree. However, elements cannot be copied and pasted between separate SysML v2 projects.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing elements' space='UAF2P']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Copy, Paste, Cut, and Delete commands in the element shortcut menu in the Containment tree.

#### Diagram Image Export

You can now preview and export images of SysML v2 diagrams and selected symbols using the Print, Print Preview, and Save as Image features.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='View image export' space='UAF2P']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Print Preview, Print and Save As Image dialogs.

#### Quick Find

Now you can locate specific elements in your model by name using the Quick Find feature. Access the Quick Find dialog through the Containment tree, Main Menu, or with a keyboard shortcut to find the needed element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Quick find' space='UAF2P']>]]>

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the Quick Find feature in the Containment tree toolbar.

#### Other Improvements

- The feature typing specialization is now available in the Diagram Palette, allowing you to create it as a path on a diagram, just like other specializations. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specializations' space='UAF2P']>]]>
- Dragging and dropping an element from the Containment tree onto the empty diagram area now instantly creates an element symbol, skipping the drag and drop selection menu for quicker symbol display. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='UAF2P']>]]>
- The Set Value drag and drop handler command is now available in the drag and drop selection menu, allowing you to set a feature element as a value for any other feature not only through the textual notation but also directly in diagrams. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Drag and Drop handler' space='UAF2P']>]]>
- You can now create any element in the Containment tree using the Create Element dialog, which you can invoke through the Create Element command in the element shortcut menu. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing elements' space='UAF2P']>]]>
- The **Show Specialization**property is now available in the Symbol Properties dialog, allowing you to specify whether specialization symbols should be displayed in the element name. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifying styles of individual symbols' space='UAF2P']>]]>
- You can now create binary connectors between elements in diagrams, even when their common owner is a non-type element or not displayed on a diagram, providing greater flexibility in connector creation.
- Now you can enable the sticky header in the Textual Editor via the 'Enable the sticky header in the editor' environment option, allowing you to display the owner of the currently visible notation at the top of the editor. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Environment options' space='UAF2P']>]]>

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.5 Version News' space='UAF2P']

#### Model Evaluation

##### Exporting Evaluation Results to CSV

You can now export evaluation results to a CSV file for further processing with external tools or for archival purposes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Exporting evaluation results to CSV.

##### Evaluation Analysis and Verification Cases

The SysML v2 Evaluation plugin can now evaluate Analysis and Verification cases and identify the best alternative using Trade Study analysis.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using Trade Study analysis.

##### Metadata Evaluation Support

Now you can query model element metadata using the standard SysML v2 syntax.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.5 Version News' space='UAF2P']

**Documentation**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML v2 Plugin Documentation' space='SYSML2PAlpha15']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

to evaluate some of the SysML v2 features that have been implemented.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><hr /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Other Improvements"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ac:link></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Model Evaluation"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ac:link></p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Exporting Evaluation Results to CSV"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Evaluation Analysis and Verification Cases"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Metadata Evaluation Support"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2><span>Full Support for SysML v2 in the Textual Editor</span></h2><p><span>The SysML v2 Textual Editor now fully supports all concepts of the SysML v2 modeling language, providing 100% coverage.</span></p><p><span>Explore every aspect of SysML v2 with confidence, as our editor meets all your modeling needs — from requirements, structure, and behavior to analysis cases, verification cases, and more.</span></p><p><span>Please note, filter conditions are not applicable for views.</span></p><h2>Rule-Based Styles</h2><p>The Alpha 1.5 version introduces new and improved rule-based styles functionality, including:</p><ul><li><strong>Predefined rule-based styles</strong>. Now the<em> Symbol Styles</em> library comes with two predefined style sheets: the improved <em>DefaultStyle </em>style sheet with updated rules and styles as well as the new <em>WhiteAndBlackStyle</em> style sheet that contains rule-based styles for representing all element symbols in black and white style. <br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Predefined style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="predefined_rule_based_styles.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);">Symbol styles represented by the <em>DefaultStyle </em>(left) and <em>WhiteAndBlackStyle </em>(right) style sheets.</span></h6><ul><li><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Custom rule-based styles</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. Instead of using the predefined styles, you can now create style sheets with your own rule-based styles either by reusing and customizing the <em>DefaultStyle</em> style sheet or creating a custom style sheet from scratch. <br /></span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Custom style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><br /><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_rule_based_styles.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The predefined rule-based symbol styles using the <em>DefaultStyle </em>style sheet (left) and an example of custom rule-based symbol styles using a custom-created style sheet (right).<br /><br /></h6></li><li><strong>Layout-sensitive changes assessment</strong>. The Symbol Properties dialog has been enhanced to track style attribute changes that can affect already-created diagrams' layout. Easily assess the highlighted properties and either confirm or deny the changes. <br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Assessing layout-sensitive changes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Layout-sensitive_changes_assessment.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />The Symbol Properties dialog displays the highlighted rows of layout-sensitive attributes whose values were updated in the style sheet. <br />At the top of the dialog, a notification provides commands for either using the new style or retaining the old one. <br />The image displays the updated <em>driver </em>part usage element symbol once the 'Use the new style' command is selected in the dialog notification.<br /><br /></h6></li><li><strong>Style sheets management</strong>. The Active Style Sheets dialog allows you to manage all existing style sheets, both predefined and custom, determining which one(s) should be activated for the project.<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Activating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Style_sheets_management.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Activating the <em>WhiteAndBlackStyle </em>style sheet instead of the <em>DefaultStyle </em>via the Active Style Sheets dialog</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></h6><h2>Specification Pane</h2><p>The Alpha 1.5 version introduces the Specification pane that can be opened for any element in the project. It allows <span>you to review and modify properties of specific elements, such as the declared name, declared short name, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="89fd7f1f-8401-4370-9e64-0cecc263b77f">type, value</ac:inline-comment-marker>, and documentation. You can also review, modify, or <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="49f8e390-d87a-49b8-9de7-9fbf570bf200">create </ac:inline-comment-marker>owned elements in the appropriate <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2b0d34ea-bbd7-42d3-91c9-745fd6401e66">compartments, which are equivalent to those displayed in diagram symbols</ac:inline-comment-marker>.<br /></span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specification_pane.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />The Specification pane displays the <em>vehicleMassLimitationRequirement </em>element's specification information.</h6><h2>States</h2><p><span>The new tool version now supports state modeling, enhancing the ability to accurately represent dynamic system behavior and improving clarity and consistency in behavioral modeling. This includes:</span></p><ul><li><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">State modeling</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">, enabling the creation of state usage and state definition elements, with support for special actions like entry, do, and exit actions. Learn more </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Creating states" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> and </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Entry, do, and exit actions for states " /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><strong>Parallel state specification</strong> for modeling concurrent behaviors. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Parallel states" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><strong>State transitions</strong>, allowing you to specify movement between states with the ability to add triggers, guard conditions, and effect actions. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Transition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><strong>Exhibit state specification</strong>, which lets you specify elements that exhibit specific states. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Exhibit state" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><br /></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="states.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The image displays a <em>Vehicle</em> part which exhibits the <em>operationalStates </em>state machine. The states include special actions like entry, do, and exit actions. The states are connected via transitions, which also contain triggers, guard conditions and effect actions. </span></h6><h2>Allocations</h2><p>The new release allows you to create allocations where <span>the responsibility for realizing the intent of the source is allocated to the target, defining traceable links as a kind of mapping across the various structures and hierarchies of a system model.</span></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Allocation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_allocation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>The image displays a <em>torqueGenAlloc</em> allocation usage feature typed by the <em>LogicalToPhysical </em>allocation definition. The allocation allocates the <em>torqueGenerator </em>part usage to the <em>powerTrain</em> part usage. Additionally, the allocation connector is also <ac:link ac:anchor="Elaborating a connector"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[elaborated]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></h6><h2 style="text-align: left;">Displaying Relationships</h2><p><span>The Display commands now include two new categories — Relationships and In-Between Relationships — for displaying relationships between element symbols on a diagram. The Relationships category contains commands for displaying the relationship paths between the selected symbol and all other symbols displayed on the diagram. The In-Between Relationships category contains commands for displaying relationship paths only between the selected symbols. The commands in these categories allow you to display all types of connectors, specializations, and other relationships (e.g., annotations, memberships, etc.).</span></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displaying_element_symbols.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">On the left: the 'Display Other' command from the In-Between Relationships display category is executed for two symbols selected on the diagram, displaying the relationship <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">(feature membership) </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">between them.<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">On the right: the 'Display Other' command from the Relationships display category is executed for the selected symbol, displaying its relationships (feature memberships) with other symbols displayed on the diagram. </span></h6><h2 style="text-align: left;">Copying and Pasting Elements</h2><p style="text-align: left;">Now you can copy, paste, cut, and delete elements using the appropriate commands in the element shortcut menu in the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="524acbe9-c70c-44c3-a1d7-ee9bcd9f6c09">Containment tree</ac:inline-comment-marker>. However, elements cannot be copied and pasted between separate SysML v2 projects.</p><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Copying_and_pasting_elements.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Copy, Paste, Cut, and Delete commands in the element shortcut menu in the Containment tree.</h6><h2>Diagram Image Export</h2><p>You can now preview and export images of SysML v2 diagrams and selected symbols using the Print, Print Preview, and Save as Image features.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="View image export" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_image_export.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Print Preview, Print and Save As Image dialogs.</h6><h2>Quick Find</h2><p>Now you can locate specific elements in your model by name using the Quick Find feature. Access the Quick Find dialog through the Containment tree, Main Menu, or with a keyboard shortcut to find the needed element.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Quick find" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="using_quick_find.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Using the Quick Find feature in the Containment tree toolbar.</h6><h2>Other Improvements</h2><ul><li>The <strong>feature typing</strong> specialization is now available in the Diagram Palette, allowing you to create it as a path on a diagram, just like other specializations. <ac:link ac:anchor="Typing an element via the diagram palette on a diagram"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Dragging and dropping an element from the Containment tree onto the empty diagram area now instantly creates an element symbol, skipping the drag and drop selection menu for quicker symbol display. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Set Value</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> drag and drop handler command is now available in the drag and drop selection menu, allowing you to set a feature element as a value for any other feature not only through the textual notation but also directly in diagrams. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Drag and Drop handler" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">You can now create any element in the Containment tree using the </span>Create Element <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">dialog, which you can invoke through the </span>Create Element <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">command in the element shortcut menu. <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating an element via the Containment tree"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The <strong>Show Specialization </strong>property is now available in the Symbol Properties dialog, allowing you to specify whether specialization symbols should be displayed in the element name.</span> <ac:link ac:anchor="The Symbol Properties dialog"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Modifying styles of individual symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">You can now create binary connectors between elements in diagrams, even when their common owner is a non-type element or not displayed on a diagram, providing greater flexibility in connector creation.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Now you can enable the sticky header in the Textual Editor via the 'Enable the sticky header in the editor' environment option, allowing you to display the owner of the currently visible notation at the top of the editor. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Environment options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ac:link></p><h2 style="text-align: left;">Model Evaluation</h2><h3>Exporting Evaluation Results to CSV</h3><p>You can now export evaluation results to a CSV file for further processing with external tools or for archival purposes.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="export_to_csv.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Exporting evaluation results to CSV.</h6><h3>Evaluation Analysis and Verification Cases</h3><p>The SysML v2 Evaluation plugin can now evaluate Analysis and Verification cases and identify the best alternative using Trade Study analysis.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="trade_strudy_analysis.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Using Trade Study analysis.</h6><h3>Metadata Evaluation Support</h3><p>Now you can query model element metadata using the standard SysML v2 syntax.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.5 Version News" /></ac:link></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Documentation</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML v2 Plugin Documentation" ri:space-key="SYSML2PAlpha15" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298090742 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00203: Alpha 1.6 Version News

- page_id: `298090742`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298090742/Alpha+1.6+Version+News
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-01-08T15:53:58.843+01:00`
- ancestors: 
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### SysML v2 Plugin

Released on:January 6, 2025

The SysML v2 Alpha 1.6 release allows you to explore and evaluate the SysML v2 features implemented to date.

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.6 Version News' space='UAF2P']**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.6 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.6 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.6 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.6 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.6 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.6 Version News' space='UAF2P'] [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.6 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.6 Version News' space='UAF2P']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.6 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.6 Version News' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.6 Version News' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.6 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.6 Version News' space='UAF2P']

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.6 Version News' space='UAF2P']**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.6 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.6 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.6 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.6 Version News' space='UAF2P']

### SysML v2 Features

#### Custom Symbolic Diagram Types

Now you can model custom symbolic diagram types as part of your projects. By defining custom view definitions, you can model symbolic diagram types with customized diagram palettes tailored to your needs. Additionally, you can use predefined buttons or model custom ones, which can be organized into categories within the custom diagram palette. This enables greater flexibility and efficiency in modeling workflows, allowing symbolic diagrams to be precisely adapted to your specific needs while remaining part of the project for which they are created.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom diagrams' space='UAF2P']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of a custom symbolic diagram type *Requirements View*modeled specifically for symbolic requirements representation. 
Its custom diagram palette includes only user-specified predefined and custom-created buttons.

#### Standard Symbolic Diagram Types

In addition to the versatile General View, three new standard symbolic diagram types - Interconnection View, Action Flow View, and State Transition View - have been introduced, each tailored for specific modeling purposes. These new symbolic diagram types feature optimized palettes with button categories designed for interconnections, action flows, and state transitions, enhancing the modeling experience.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with views' space='UAF2P']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays General View, Interconnection View, State Transition View, and Action Flow View symbolic diagram types.

#### Create Relationship Command

You can now create relationships using the Create Relationship command, accessible from the element shortcut menu for both elements in the Containment tree and element symbols on a diagram. This provides an additional way to create relationships, making it especially convenient for working directly within the Containment tree and creating relationships between element symbols on separate diagrams.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing elements' space='UAF2P']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays the creation of a connection usage between two elements via the Create Relationship command in the element shortcut menu.

#### Dependency Relationship

You can now model dependencies using the dependency relationship, allowing you to represent how changes to supplier elements may affect client elements. Binary and n-ary dependencies can be created via textual notation and symbolic diagrams, offering flexibility and clarity in modeling inter-element relationships and supporting traceability across abstraction levels.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space='UAF2P']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays a *Use*dependency where the package *Service Layer*is the client (source) and*Data Layer*is the supplier (target). The supplier end of the dependency path is indicated with an arrowhead.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed n-ary dependency where the package *Service Layer*is the client (source). The packages*Data Layer*and*External Interface Layer*are the supplier (target).The supplier end of the dependency path is indicated with an arrowhead.

#### Derivation Connection

You can now model requirements derivation using the derivation connection. This feature allows you to link higher-level requirements to more detailed, derived ones, establishing clear traceability and hierarchy in your specifications. Both binary and n-ary derivation connections can be created via textual notation and symbolic diagrams, enabling flexible and efficient requirements management.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deriving requirements' space='UAF2P']>]]>

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The image displays binary and n-ary derivation connectors between original and derived requirements with the appropriate labels at each connector end.

#### Easier Access to Used Project Actions

Now you can quickly access used project-related actions, such as **Open** and **Remove**, directly from the shortcut menu in the Containment tree of a project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening a used project directly from the Containment tree.

#### Element Navigation Improvements

Element navigation has been improved with support for navigating among elements based on connectors between them, such as connections, successions, interfaces, etc. Using the Go To command, you can now seamlessly navigate to related elements via their connectors, further enhancing the efficiency of locating elements based on their relationships.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element navigation' space='UAF2P']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Go To command's submenu displays elements related to the *vccu*element. Clicking the *'part vccu'* element under the Subsetting Feature category displays the *vccu*part element that subsets the *vccu*element for which the command was invoked.

#### Specification Panel Improvements

The Alpha 1.6 version brings several enhancements to the Specification Panel:

- New Properties Added: The Properties section now includes 'Performed Action,' 'Use Case Included,' 'Exhibited State,' and 'Result Expression' property fields for specific element types. It allows you to easily specify the performed actions, included use cases, and exhibited states for appropriate elements using the Drag and Drop handler. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space='UAF2P']>]]>
- Improved Feature Value Editing: Feature value modification has been optimized for improved efficiency. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space='UAF2P']>]]>
- Feature Multiplicity: You can now specify feature multiplicity. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space='UAF2P']>]]>
- Inherited Features Display: The Compartments section now shows inherited features in addition to directly owned ones, with support for redefinition. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space='UAF2P']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The highlighted areas display feature value editing, feature multiplicity editing, owned and inheritedfeatures indication.

#### Textual Editor Improvements

The Textual Editor has been enhanced with the following updates.

###### Significantly Improved Textual Editor Performance

The Textual Editor’s performance has been significantly enhanced through improvements in name resolution functionality. These improvements reduce processing times and provide a smoother and more efficient modeling experience in the Textual Editor.

###### Improved Copy-Pasting of Textual Notation from the Textual Editor

Display IDs are now automatically removed when copying textual notation from the Textual Editor and pasting it elsewhere (e.g., in a notepad). This enhancement eliminates the need to switch to read-only mode or manually remove Display IDs after pasting, simplifying the process and saving time.

#### Known Issues

- An internal error may occur when the 'Collaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE Platform' is enabled and a SysML v2 project is opened. This issue arises if the Magic Systems Cybersecurity Designer Plugin or Cameo Systems Cybersecurity Designer Plugin is installed, but it may also occur in other scenarios.

### SysML v2 Evaluation Features

#### Textual Representation Support for Calculations

The SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin now supports textual representation elements allowing you to evaluate scripts defined in other script languages (similar to Opaque Actions in SysML v1). Supported languages include: Javascript, Groovy, BeanShell, Python (Jython).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Textual representation support.

#### Updated Verification Annotations

Now deeply nested failures can be highlighted in yellow, making it easier to identify issues within complex model structures. New tooltips provide clear identification of the failing components. Additionally, the new evaluation project options allow you to customize how requirement and constraint verification results are displayed in diagrams for better clarity and control. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Evaluating requirements in diagrams' space='SYSML2EPAlpha16']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Verification annotations in a diagram.

#### Error Messages

The SysML v2 Model Evaluation panel now displays error messages to help identify and solve evaluation issues.

#### Key Fixes

- Units specified in a model no longer break the evaluation.
- Additional KerML functions have been implemented. Classification expressions, Trigonometric, Sequence, Rational, Natural, Integer, Boolean, and Real functions are now fully supported.

**Documentation**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML v2 Plugin Documentation' space='SYSML2PAlpha16']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin Documentation' space='SYSML2EPAlpha16']

/></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1>SysML v2 Features</h1><h2>Custom Symbolic Diagram Types</h2><p>Now you can model custom symbolic diagram types as part of your projects. <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">By defining custom view definitions, you can model symbolic diagram types with customized diagram palettes tailored to your needs. Additionally, you can use predefined buttons or model custom ones, which can be organized into categories within the custom diagram palette. This enables greater flexibility and efficiency in modeling workflows, allowing symbolic diagrams to be precisely adapted to your specific needs while remaining part of the project for which they are created.</span></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Custom diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_diagrams.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.6 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of a custom symbolic diagram type <em>Requirements View </em>modeled specifically for symbolic requirements representation. <br />Its custom diagram palette includes only user-specified predefined and custom-created buttons.<span style="text-align: left;font-weight: 400;letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span></h6><p><br /></p><h2>Standard Symbolic Diagram Types</h2><p>In addition to the versatile General View, three new standard symbolic diagram types - Interconnection View, Action Flow View, and State Transition View - have been introduced, each tailored for specific modeling purposes. These new symbolic diagram types feature optimized palettes with button categories designed for interconnections, action flows, and state transitions, enhancing the modeling experience.</p><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbolic diagram types"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Working with views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_kinds.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.6 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays General View, Interconnection View, State Transition View, and Action Flow View symbolic diagram types.</h6><h2>Create Relationship Command</h2><p>You can now create relationships using the Create Relationship command, accessible from the element shortcut menu for both elements in the Containment tree and element symbols on a diagram. This provides an additional way to create relationships, making it especially convenient for working directly within the Containment tree <span>and creating relationships between element symbols on separate diagrams</span>.</p><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating a relationship element via the Create Relationship command"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_relationship.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.6 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays the creation of a connection usage between two elements via the Create Relationship command in the element shortcut menu.</h6><h2><span>Dependency Relationship</span></h2><p>You can now model dependencies using the dependency relationship, allowing you to represent how changes to supplier elements may affect client elements. Binary and n-ary dependencies can be created via textual notation and symbolic diagrams, offering flexibility and clarity in modeling inter-element relationships and supporting traceability across abstraction levels.</p><p><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Dependency" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dependency.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.6 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays a <em>Use </em>dependency where the package <em>Service Layer </em><span>is the client (source) and </span><em>Data Layer </em><span>is the supplier (target). The supplier end of the dependency path is indicated with an arrowhead.</span></h6></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="nary_dependencies.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.6 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays an unnamed n-ary dependency where the package <em>Service Layer </em><span>is the client (source). The packages </span><em>Data Layer </em><span>and </span><em>External Interface Layer </em><span>are the supplier (target). </span><span>The supplier end of the dependency path is indicated with an arrowhead.</span></h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2><span>Derivation Connection</span></h2><p>You can now model requirements derivation using the derivation connection. This feature allows you to link higher-level requirements to more detailed, derived ones, establishing clear traceability and hierarchy in your specifications. Both binary and n-ary derivation connections can be created via textual notation and symbolic diagrams, enabling flexible and efficient requirements management.</p><p><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Deriving requirements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="requirements_derivation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.6 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The image displays binary and n-ary derivation connectors between original and derived requirements with the appropriate labels at each connector end.</span></h6><h2><span>Easier Access to Used Project Actions</span></h2><p>Now you can quickly access used project-related actions, such as <strong>Open</strong> and <strong>Remove</strong>, directly from the shortcut menu in the Containment tree of a project.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="opening_used_project.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.6 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Opening a used project directly from the Containment tree.</h6><h2>Element Navigation Improvements</h2><p>Element navigation has been improved with support for navigating among elements based on connectors between them, such as connections, successions, interfaces, etc. Using the Go To command, you can now seamlessly navigate to related elements via their connectors, further enhancing the efficiency of locating elements based on their relationships.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Element navigation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_navigation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.6 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Go To command's submenu displays elements related to the <em>vccu </em>element. Clicking the <em>'part vccu'</em> element under the Subsetting Feature category displays the <em>vccu </em>part element that subsets the <em>vccu </em>element for which the command was invoked.  </h6><h2>Specification Panel Improvements</h2><p><span>The Alpha 1.6 version brings several enhancements to the Specification Panel:</span></p><ul><li><strong>New Properties Added:</strong> The Properties section now includes 'Performed Action,' 'Use Case Included,' 'Exhibited State,' and 'Result Expression' property fields for specific element types. It allows you to easily specify the performed actions, included use cases, and exhibited states for appropriate elements using the Drag and Drop handler. <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying element properties via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><strong>Improved Feature Value Editing:</strong> Feature value modification has been optimized for improved efficiency. <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the feature value via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><strong>Feature Multiplicity:</strong><span> You can now specify feature multiplicity. </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the multiplicity of the element via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><strong>Inherited Features Display:</strong> The Compartments section now shows inherited features in addition to directly owned ones, with support for redefinition. <ac:link ac:anchor="Redefining inherited elements via the compartment in the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="specification_panel_improvements.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.6 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The highlighted areas display feature value editing, feature multiplicity editing, owned and <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">inherited </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">features indication.</span></h6><h2>Textual Editor Improvements</h2><p><span>The Textual Editor has been enhanced with the following updates.</span></p><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Significantly Improved Textual Editor Performance </h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span>The Textual Editor’s performance has been significantly enhanced through improvements in name resolution functionality. These improvements reduce processing times and provide a smoother and more efficient modeling experience in the Textual Editor.</span></p><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span>Improved Copy-Pasting of Textual Notation from the Textual Editor</span></h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span>Display IDs are now automatically removed when copying textual notation from the Textual Editor and pasting it elsewhere (e.g., in a notepad). This enhancement eliminates the need to switch to read-only mode or manually remove Display IDs after pasting, simplifying the process and saving time.</span></p><h2>Known Issues</h2><ul><li>An internal error may occur when the 'Collaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE Platform' is enabled and a SysML v2 project is opened. This issue arises if the Magic Systems Cybersecurity Designer Plugin or Cameo Systems Cybersecurity Designer Plugin is installed, but it may also occur in other scenarios.</li></ul><h1>SysML v2 Evaluation Features</h1><h2>Textual Representation Support for Calculations</h2><p>The SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin now supports textual representation elements allowing you to evaluate scripts defined in other script languages (similar to Opaque Actions in SysML v1). Supported languages include: Javascript, Groovy, BeanShell, Python (Jython).</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="textual_notation_support.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.6 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Textual representation support.</h6><h2>Updated Verification Annotations</h2><p>Now deeply nested failures can be highlighted in yellow, making it easier to identify issues within complex model structures. New tooltips provide clear identification of the failing components. Additionally, the new evaluation project options allow you to customize how requirement and constraint verification results are displayed in diagrams for better clarity and control. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2EPAlpha16" ri:content-title="Evaluating requirements in diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="verification_annotations.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.6 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Verification annotations in a diagram.</h6><h2>Error Messages</h2><p>The SysML v2 Model Evaluation panel now displays error messages to help identify and solve evaluation issues.</p><h2>Key Fixes</h2><ul><li>Units specified in a model no longer break the evaluation.</li><li>Additional KerML functions have been implemented. Classification expressions, Trigonometric, Sequence, Rational, Natural, Integer, Boolean, and Real functions are now fully supported.</li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Documentation</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML v2 Plugin Documentation" ri:space-key="SYSML2PAlpha16" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML v2 Alpha 1.6]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2EPAlpha16" ri:content-title="SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML v2 Evaluation Alpha 1.6]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298090671 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00204: Alpha 1.7 Version News

- page_id: `298090671`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298090671/Alpha+1.7+Version+News
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-17T15:07:10.783+02:00`
- ancestors: 
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### SysML v2 Plugin

Released on:March 17, 2025

The SysML v2 Alpha 1.7 release allows you to explore and evaluate the SysML v2 features implemented to date.

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.7 Version News' space='UAF2P']**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.7 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.7 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.7 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.7 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.7 Version News' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.7 Version News' space='UAF2P']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.7 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.7 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.7 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.7 Version News' space='UAF2P']

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.7 Version News' space='UAF2P']**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.7 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.7 Version News' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alpha 1.7 Version News' space='UAF2P']

### SysML v2 Features

##### SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration

This release introduces the SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration plugin, which provides model transformation capabilities, allowing you to transform SysML v1 models into SysML v2. The plugin is included in the *core.rdzip* file alongside other SysML v2 plugins. Currently, not all SysML v1 elements are supported for migration, as the plugin is a work in progress. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migration from SysML v1 to SysML v2' space='UAF2P']>]]>

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The image displays a SysML v1 model and its result after migration to SysML v2.

##### Tabular Views

You can now create tabular views as part of your projects. This update introduces two types of tables — Generic Table and Requirements Table — each with predefined columns that display key information about exposed model elements, such as names, owners, documentation, and more, for easy review. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tabular views' space='UAF2P']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of a Generic table.

##### View Creation Using Expressions

- Now, you can specify filter conditions for views to specify which elements should be exposed by the view, providing you more control over the views you create and the element symbols they display. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering elements for views' space='UAF2P']>]]>

packageMetadataDefinitions{ 
metadatadefSafety{ 
attributeisMandatory:Boolean; 
} 
}

packageVehicle{ 
privateimportMetadataDefinitions::**; 
partvehicle{ 
partinterior{ 
partalarm; 
partseatBelt[2] { @Safety{isMandatory=true;} } 
partfrontSeat[2]; 
partdriverAirBag{ @Safety{isMandatory=false;} } 
} 
} 
}

// exposes elements with *Safety*metadata attribute *isMandatory* value *true*from the exposed package *Vehicle*: 
view'Vehicle Features':'SysML Standard Diagrams'::gv{ 
exposeVehicle::**; 
filter@MetadataDefinitions::SafetyandMetadataDefinitions::Safety::isMandatory; 
}

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Use a rendering feature to specify how a particular view and its exposed elements should be displayed for viewing, such as tree-view or nested-view style. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rendering views' space='UAF2P']>]]>

partemployeeInformation{ 
partcandidateData; 
partemployeeData{ 
itememployeeName; 
} 
}

// an undefined view with recursive membership exposition filtering down to part usages and filtering out KerML library elements, and the *Views::asTreeDiagram* renderer, specifying the element display in tree-view style: 
view'Employee Information (tree-view)'{ 
exposeemployeeInformation::**; 
filter@SysML::PartUsage; 
filterKerML::Element::isLibraryElement==false; 
renderViews::asTreeDiagram; 
} 
// an undefined view with recursive membership exposition filtering down to part usages and filtering out KerML library elements, and the *Views::asInterconnectionDiagram* renderer, specifying the element display in nested-view style: 
view'Employee Information (nested-view)'{ 
exposeemployeeInformation::**; 
filter@SysML::PartUsage; 
filterKerML::Element::isLibraryElement==false; 
renderViews::asInterconnectionDiagram; 
}

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- You can easily display exposed element symbols on symbolic views using the Display Exposed Elements command. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='UAF2P']>]]>

partemployeeInformation{ 
partcandidateData; 
partemployeeData{ 
itememployeeName; 
} 
}

// a view defined by the standard predefined view definition *General View* (that contains a *'SysML Standard Diagrams'::asGraphicalDiagram*renderer) with recursive membership exposition filtering down to part usages and filtering out KerML library elements: 
view'Employee Information':'SysML Standard Diagrams'::gv{ 
exposeemployeeInformation::**; 
filter@SysML::PartUsage; 
filterKerML::Element::isLibraryElement==false; 
}

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- For your convenience, the *CustomViewDefinitions*library package containing custom predefined view definitions is added to the modeling tool. These view definitions c ontain common element filters narrowing down exposed elements eliminating the need for you to write your own filters. To learn more, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbolic views' space='UAF2P'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tabular views' space='UAF2P'] pages. You may also review the *Views.mdzip* project sample available with the modeling tool.

partemployeeInformation{ 
partcandidateData; 
partemployeeData{ 
itememployeeName; 
} 
}

// a view defined by the custom predefined view definition *PartsNestedView* with recursive membership exposition. The *PartsNestedView* view definition already contains filters to narrow down the exposed elements to part usages and exclude KerML library elements, and the *Views::asInterconnectionDiagram* renderer: 
view'Employee Information':'CustomViewDefinitions'::PartsNestedView{ 
exposeemployeeInformation::**; 
}

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Display Features Recursively Commands

Display commands are now supplemented with Display Features Recursively, Display Parts Recursively, and Display Actions Recursively commands, allowing you to display all owned and inherited features, parts, or actionsrecursively.[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='UAF2P']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Enhanced Graphical Support

- Expanded Compartments Support

Compartments for parts, ports, and include use cases are now represented in the graphical notation. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='UAF2P']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Updated Interface Graphical Notation

The graphical notation for the interface usage is now updated. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space='UAF2P']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Rule-Based Symbol Styles Improvements

- Rule-based symbol styles functionality has been enhanced with additional condition types:
  - ByMetadataAnnotationCondition . Element appearance in a view (i.e. applied symbol style) can now be predicated on the type(s) of metadata annotation(s), analogous to style-for-stereotype in SysML v1.x terminology. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P']>]]>

packageNewStylesPackage{privateimportStyles::**;privateimportPolicies::*;privateimportModel::**;partdefNewStyle:>StyleSheet{partmetadataApprovalRule:>rule{part:>>condition:ByMetadataAnnotationCondition{ // the rule's condition is specified as *ByMetadataAnnotationCondition*, targeting symbols by element metadatarefitem:>>annotatingMetadataType= Safety.metadata; // the condition targets *Safety* metadata}view:ShapeStyle:>>style{attribute:>>fillColordefault"#B2BFFF";}}}}

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- 
  - ByDiagramTypeCondition . Element appearance can now depend in the view type, allowing the same element to be displayed differently on various standard and custom view types. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P']>]]>

- Stylesheets can now include a global condition, in addition to each rule having separate conditions. This is particularly useful when combined with ByDiagramTypeCondition for creating stylesheets tailored to specific view types. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P']>]]>

// the following style sheet contains a global condition 'General View Condition' followed by an individual rule for a specific element metaclass and applicable style

packageNewStylesPackage{ 
privateimportStyles::**; 
privateimportPolicies::*; 
partdefNewStyle:>StyleSheet{ 
/* ------- global condition -------*/ 
part'General View Condition':>>styleSheetCondition:ByDiagramTypeCondition{ // global style sheet condition targeting symbols by view (diagram type) 
refitem:>>diagramType='SysML Standard Diagrams'::gv.metadata; // the condition targets *General View* views 
} 
/* -----------------------------------*/ 
partpartRule:>rule{ // an individual rule targets *PartUsage* metaclasses and applies the specified style to them. Because of the global rule, only part usages displayed on a General View will be affected by the rule 
part:>>condition:ByElementMetaclassCondition{ 
refitem:>>elementMetaclass= SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata; 
} 
view:ShapeStyle:>>style{ 
attribute:>>fillColordefault"#B2BFFF"; 
} 
} 
} 
}

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Quality-of-life improvements include multi-selection for managing styles of multiple symbols at once and background processing of style rule calculations for better performance.

##### Specification Panel Improvements

- Now, the metadata compartment organizes the features of the applied metadata, grouping them under their respective metadata labels. These displayed features include inherited features with default values and redefined features, the same way as in element symbols on views. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space='UAF2P']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- You can now redefine inherited features from applied metadata directly through the Specification panel. This functionality simplifies the process of redefining inherited metadata features, enabling efficient redefinition without the need for the Textual Editor. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space='UAF2P']>]]>
  - Clicking the Add button in the metadata compartment's bar displays a submenu with the applied metadatas and the inherited features without a set default value. Clicking a feature redefines it for the element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- 
  - To avoid global model changes, clicking the Navigate button next to the feature inherited from an applied metadata with a set default value opens the feature in the Specification panel in read-only mode, where you can click the Redefine button to redefine the feature, if you need to change the default value in the selected context.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The Specification panel now includes the Navigation tab, which allows you to easily navigate from a selected element to its related elements based on their relationships. The tab organizes related elements into categories, such as Subsetted Feature, Redefined Feature, Flow, Connection, Type, and more, clearly indicating the nature of their relationships. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space='UAF2P']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Other Improvements

- The default font in the modeling tool has been changed from Arial to Roboto, resolving previous letter spacing issues and improving text readability. Please note that on macOS, certain non-Latin characters — such as those used in Chinese or Japanese — may not render correctly.
- New SysML v2 Textual Editor environment options have been introduced:
  - *Display Metadata as Keywords* allows you to specify whether metadata should be displayed as keywords or symbols in the textual notation.
  - *Use Short Name Instead of Name for Requirement* allows you to specify whether therequirement's name or short name should be displayed in the textual notation.
  - *Max Column for Hard Wrap* allows you to s pecify the maximum number of columns for hard wrapping lines in the Textual Editor. When a line exceeds the specified limit, it is split into sections of new indented lines to fit the line length based on the maximum number of columns. When wrapping, the editor follows specific rules, such as breaking at keywords ("connect", "from", "to", "of"), binary operators ("+", "-"), commas, and long single-line bodies. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Environment options' space='UAF2P']>]]>

### SysML v2 Evaluation Features

##### Manipulating Initial Runtime Values

You can now use diagrams to modify initial runtime values during evaluation. Editable values are indicated by the ":=" sign during runtime, meaning they are not bound and can be adjusted for evaluation purposes. When an initial runtime value is changed, all evaluation results are automatically recalculated and requirements are reverified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Changing initial runtime values in a diagram.

##### MATLAB Integration Support

The SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin now supports integration with MATLAB, enabling you to use MATLAB scripts for calculations. In the previous release, we introduced textual representation elements, allowing the use of various scripting languages. With this release, once MATLAB integration is set up, you can incorporate MATLAB as an additional scripting language.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of using MATLAB scripts for calculations.

##### Sequence Function Support

With this release, sequence functions are fully supported.

**Documentation**

- SysML v2 Alpha 1.7
- [ATTACHMENT filename='matlab_integration.png']
- SysML v2 Collaborative Modeling 1.7 [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML v2 Collaborative Modeling Documentation' space='SYSML2CM']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

/></p><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1>SysML v2 Features</h1><h3>SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration</h3><p>This release introduces the SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration plugin, which provides model transformation capabilities, allowing you to transform SysML v1 models into SysML v2. The plugin is included in the <em>core.rdzip</em> file alongside other SysML v2 plugins. Currently, not all SysML v1 elements are supported for migration, as the plugin is a work in progress. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Migration from SysML v1 to SysML v2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.7 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The image displays a SysML v1 model and its result after migration to SysML v2.</h6><h3>Tabular Views</h3><p>You can now create tabular views as part of your projects. This update introduces two types of tables — Generic Table and Requirements Table — <span>each with predefined columns that display key information about exposed model elements, such as names, owners, documentation, and more, for easy review. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="tabular_view_example.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.7 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An example of a Generic table.</span></h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>View Creation Using Expressions</h3><ul><li><span>Now, you can specify filter <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9229e5dc-45eb-4d9a-8d86-be3cde866796">conditions</ac:inline-comment-marker> for views to specify which elements should be exposed by the view, providing you more control over the views you create and the element symbols they display. </span><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Filtering elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section
