style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">employeeInformation</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;  </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">@ </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysML::PartUsage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">KerML::Element::isLibraryElement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> == </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">Views::asTreeDiagram</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// an undefined view with recursive membership exposition filtering down to part usages and filtering out KerML library elements, and the <em>Views::asInterconnectionDiagram</em> renderer, specifying the element display in nested-view style:</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Employee Information (nested-view)'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">employeeInformation</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;  </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">@ </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysML::PartUsage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">KerML::Element::isLibraryElement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> == </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">Views::asInterconnectionDiagram</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="renderings.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.7 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ul><li>You can easily display exposed element symbols on symbolic views using the <span>Display Exposed </span><span>Elements command. </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4ec32ca0-3d01-4bd5-bb17-387bcb302e45"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">employeeInformation</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">candidateData</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">employeeData</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">employeeName</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a view defined by the standard predefined view definition <em>General View</em> (that contains a <em style="text-align: left;">'SysML Standard Diagrams'::asGraphicalDiagram </em>renderer) with recursive membership exposition filtering down to part usages and filtering out KerML library elements:</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Employee Information'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'SysML Standard Diagrams'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::gv</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">employeeInformation</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;   </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">@ </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysML::PartUsage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">KerML::Element::isLibraryElement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> == </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display_Exposed_Elements_command.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.7 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ul><li><span>For your convenience, the <em>CustomViewDefinitions </em>library package containing custom predefined view definitions is added to the modeling tool. These view definitions c</span>ontain common element filters<span> narrowing down exposed elements eliminating the need for you to write your own filters. To learn more, see the <span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Symbolic views" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Tabular views" /></ac:link> pages. You may also review the <em>Views.mdzip</em> project sample available with the modeling tool. </span></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df9f8ace-8f72-401c-b05c-65321dc20e7b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">employeeInformation</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">candidateData</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">employeeData</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">employeeName</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a view defined by the custom predefined view definition <em>PartsNestedView</em> with recursive membership exposition. The <em>PartsNestedView</em> view definition already contains filters to narrow down the exposed elements to part usages and exclude KerML library elements, and the <em>Views::asInterconnectionDiagram</em> renderer:</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Employee Information'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'CustomViewDefinitions'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::PartsNestedView</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">employeeInformation</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_predefined_view_definitions.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.7 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3><span>Display Features Recursively Commands</span></h3><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>Display commands are now supplemented with Display Features Recursively, Display Parts Recursively, and Display Actions Recursively commands, allowing you to display <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> all owned and inherited features, parts, or actions </span>recursively. </span></span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:image ac:align="right"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display_Features_Commands.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.7 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display_Features_Recursively_Commands.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.7 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Enhanced Graphical Support</h3><ul><li><h4>Expanded Compartments Support</h4></li></ul><p><span>Compartments for parts, ports, and include use cases are now represented in the graphical notation. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="compartments.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.7 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ul><li><h4>Updated Interface Graphical Notation</h4></li></ul><p>The graphical notation for the interface usage is now updated. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="interface.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.7 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Rule-Based Symbol Styles Improvements</h3><ul><li>Rule-based symbol styles functionality has been enhanced with additional condition types:<ul><li><em>ByMetadataAnnotationCondition</em>. Element appearance in a view (i.e. applied symbol style) can now be predicated on the type(s) of metadata annotation(s), analogous to style-for-stereotype in SysML v1.x terminology. <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying conditions for a rule"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Creating style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54ec3c1e-f836-4456-9c0a-556a564fff5c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStylesPackage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Styles</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Policies</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::*;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Model</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">StyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">metadataApprovalRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByMetadataAnnotationCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the rule's condition is specified as <em>ByMetadataAnnotationCondition</em>, targeting symbols by element metadata<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">annotatingMetadataType</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = Safety.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the condition targets <em>Safety</em> metadata<br /></span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            }<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fillColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#B2BFFF&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            }<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ByMetadataAnnotationCondition.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.7 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>ByDiagramTypeCondition</em>. Element appearance can now depend in the view type, allowing the same element to be displayed differently on various standard and custom view types. <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying conditions for a rule"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Creating style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ul><li>Stylesheets can now include a global condition, in addition to each rule having separate conditions. This is particularly useful when combined with <em>ByDiagramTypeCondition</em> for creating stylesheets tailored to specific view types. <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating global conditions for a style sheet"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Creating style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f7ba2d36-872c-4fcb-8846-b906891989bc"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the following style sheet contains a global condition 'General View Condition' followed by an individual rule for a specific element metaclass and applicable style</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStylesPackage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Styles</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Policies</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::*;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">StyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">        /* ------- <span>global condition ------- </span>*/</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'General View Condition'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">styleSheetCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByDiagramTypeCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// global style sheet condition targeting symbols by view (diagram type)</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">diagramType</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">'SysML Standard Diagrams'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::gv.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the condition targets <em>General View</em> views</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">        /* ----------------------------<span>------- </span>*/ </span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// an individual rule targets <span><em>PartUsage</em> metaclasses and applies the specified style to them. Because of the global rule, only part usages displayed on a General View will be affected by the rule</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {  </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">elementMetaclass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fillColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#B2BFFF&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="global_condition.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.7 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ul><li><p>Quality-of-life improvements include multi-selection for managing styles of multiple symbols at once and background processing of style rule calculations for better performance.</p></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Specification Panel Improvements</h3><ul><li>Now, the metadata compartment organizes the features of the applied metadata, grouping them under their respective metadata labels. <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e4804c06-dfb7-44d8-aeca-b4552c539ccf">These displayed features include inherited features with default values and redefined features, the same way as in element symbols on views. <ac:link ac:anchor="Redefining inherited metadata features via the compartment in the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="grouped_applied_metadata.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.7 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ul><li>You can now redefine inherited features from applied metadata directly through the Specification panel. This functionality simplifies the process of redefining inherited metadata features, enabling efficient redefinition without the need for the Textual Editor. <ac:link ac:anchor="Redefining inherited metadata features via the compartment in the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><ul><li> Clicking the Add button in the metadata compartment's bar displays a submenu with the applied metadatas and the inherited features without a set default value. Clicking a feature redefines it for the element.</li></ul></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="redefining_inherited_metadata_features_1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.7 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cda182c2-17fd-4e0c-8f15-5c366cbcd76e">To avoid global model changes, clicking the Navigate button next to the feature inherited from an applied metadata with a set default value opens the feature in the Specification panel in read-only mode, where you can click the Redefine button to redefine the feature, if you need to change the default value in the selected context.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="redefining_inherited_metadata_features_2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.7 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ul><li>The Specification panel now includes the Navigation tab, which allows you to easily navigate from a selected element to its related elements based on their relationships. The tab organizes related elements into categories, such as Subsetted Feature, Redefined Feature, Flow, Connection, Type, and more, clearly indicating the nature of their relationships. <ac:link ac:anchor="Navigation tab"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Navigation_tab.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.7 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Other Improvements</h3><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span>The default font in the modeling tool has been changed from Arial to Roboto, resolving previous letter spacing issues and improving text readability. Please note that on macOS, certain non-Latin characters — such as those used in Chinese or Japanese — may not render correctly.</span></span> </li><li>New SysML v2 Textual Editor environment options have been introduced:<br /><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><em>Display Metadata as Keywords</em> allows you to specify whether metadata should be <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">displayed as keywords or symbols in the textual notation.</span></span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><em>Use Short Name Instead of Name for Requirement</em> <span>allows you to specify whether the </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">requirement's name or short name should be displayed in the textual notation.</span></span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><em>Max Column for Hard Wrap</em> allows you to s</span>pecify the maximum number of columns for hard wrapping lines in the Textual Editor. When a line exceeds the specified limit, it is split into sections of new indented lines to fit the line length based on the maximum number of columns. When wrapping, the editor follows specific rules, such as breaking at keywords (&quot;connect&quot;, &quot;from&quot;, &quot;to&quot;, &quot;of&quot;), binary operators (&quot;+&quot;, &quot;-&quot;), commas, and long single-line bodies. <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <br /></span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Environment options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1>SysML v2 Evaluation Features</h1><h3>Manipulating Initial Runtime Values</h3><p><span>You can now use diagrams to modify initial runtime values during evaluation. Editable values are indicated by the &quot;:=&quot; sign during runtime, meaning they are not bound and can be adjusted for evaluation purposes. When an initial runtime value is changed, all evaluation results are automatically recalculated and requirements are reverified.</span></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_runtime_values.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.7 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>Changing initial runtime values in a diagram.</span></h6><h3>MATLAB Integration Support</h3><p>The SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin now supports integration with MATLAB, enabling you to use MATLAB scripts for calculations. In the previous release, we introduced textual representation elements, allowing the use of various scripting languages. With this release, once MATLAB integration is set up, you can incorporate MATLAB as an additional scripting language.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="matlab_integration.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.7 Version News" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of using MATLAB scripts for calculations.</h6><h3>Sequence Function Support</h3><p>With this release, sequence functions are fully supported.</p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Documentation</strong></p><ul><li>SysML v2 Alpha 1.7</li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="matlab_integration.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="Alpha 1.7 Version News" /></ri:attachment><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML v2 Evaluation Alpha 1.7]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>SysML v2 Collaborative Modeling 1.7 <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2CM" ri:content-title="SysML v2 Collaborative Modeling Documentation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````
